title: Repository Guidelines
text: R/ organized durable workflow domains. Current modules include preprocessing (preprocessing.R), clustering/integration (analysis_clustering.R), differential expression (analysis_de.R), enrichment (analysis_enrichment.R), metrics/composition (analysis_metrics.R), example data (data_examples.R), IO (data_io.R), interpretation (interpretation.R), package helpers (package_tools.R), signatures (signatures.R), visualization (visualization.R), small utilities (utils.R). man/ contains roxygen2-generated .Rd files. Treat generated output keep synchronized roxygen comments R/. tests/testthat/ currently small unit-test surface; add focused tests near behavior change. vignettes/ contains longer workflows. Keep chunks check-safe avoid unconditional network access heavyweight setup examples. data/ stores package datasets. docs/codex/ stores package-maintainer Codex docs modernization memory; already excluded package builds via .Rbuildignore. inst/codex/project-template/ stores shipped initialized-project governance template. inst/codex/package-skills/ stores shipped package-usage Codex skills. Keep repository-planning modernization memory installed user assets. _pkgdown.yml .github/workflows/ define package website CI entry points.

title: Repository Guidelines
text: Rscript -e 'testthat::test_local(stop_on_failure = TRUE)' runs local test suite. Rscript -e 'testthat::test_local(filter = "composition", stop_on_failure = TRUE)' runs current focused tests iterating composition-related changes. Rscript -e '(requireNamespace("devtools", quietly = TRUE)) devtools::document() else stop("devtools installed")' regenerates NAMESPACE man/ roxygen changes. R CMD build . builds package tarball. R CMD check ---manual Shennong_*.tar.gz full package check successful build. local environments without optional Suggests, use _R_CHECK_FORCE_SUGGESTS_=false R CMD check ---manual Shennong_*.tar.gz. Rscript scripts/check-prepush.R --filter="deconvolution" --quick runs fast edit-loop check: targeted tests, source build, structural R CMD check without re-running examples/vignettes/full tests. Rscript scripts/check-prepush.R --filter="deconvolution" runs standard local pre-push path. avoids re-running tests inside R CMD check test_local() unless --check-tests supplied.

title: Repository Guidelines
text: Preserve public behavior unless change explicitly justified, documented, validated. Prefer small, reviewable refactors repo-wide rewrites. Keep exported user-facing functions strict sn_verb_noun naming family. introduce camelCase, dot.case, mixed prefixes. Reuse existing sn_* naming families whenever possible. Keep internal helpers unexported clearly named. Prefer explicit namespace usage roxygen @importFrom entries hidden dependencies. Keep examples deterministic safe package checks; wrap network- dependency-heavy examples \dontrun{} make conditional. Avoid introducing new package dependencies unless remove concrete maintenance problem solved simply.

title: Repository Guidelines
text: Add update tests changing behavior risky areas. Prefer lightweight tests require external downloads optional heavyweight packages unless function contract truly depends . Run narrowest relevant tests first, rerun full local suite closing milestone. roxygen, exports, package metadata change, regenerate documentation rerun relevant validation commands. add change user-facing function, parameter, stored-result schema, workflow, must also update relevant pkgdown article(s) shipped Codex assets inst/codex/project-template/ inst/codex/package-skills/ change set affected. user-facing change set must also update NEWS.md release notes reflect shipped behavior. New stored-result retrieval interpretation features must documented user level least one concrete example showing discover stored result retrieve Seurat object. updating user-facing feature, rebuild pkgdown locally rendered site matches current package sources. Updating vignette/reference source files sufficient.

title: Repository Guidelines
text: Keep modernization step self-contained: one small change set, one validation pass, one update docs/codex/Status.md docs/codex/Decisions.md. overwrite unrelated working tree changes; repository may dirty. Record compatibility notes, validation commands, rationale non-obvious changes docs/codex/. Breaking changes require explicit documentation docs/codex/Decisions.md NEWS.md. Use Conventional Commits commit messages. Preferred commit format: <type>(<scope>): <summary>. Typical types repo: feat, fix, refactor, docs, test, build, ci, chore. Keep summary imperative specific, example: feat(clustering): consolidate quick integration workflows. change breaking, add ! type scope explain break commit body NEWS.md.

title: Repository Guidelines
text: Likely public API surface: IO (sn_read, sn_write, dataset loading), Seurat initialization/normalization/QC, clustering/integration/annotation, plotting, signatures/enrichment, composition/metrics. Current high-risk zones data import/export, heavyweight optional-package integrations, schema stored object@misc. Current modernization order : inventory API, stabilize tests, normalize metadata namespace handling, reorganize source files without changing behavior, tighten internals documentation. Repository-internal developer memory stays docs/codex/ AGENTS.md. Installed-package user assets belong inst/codex/project-template/ inst/codex/package-skills/.

title: Contributing to Shennong
text: Use small, reviewable changes. Prefer one focused behavior change per commit, matching tests documentation updates. opening pull request, run: shell: standard local pre-push path, can use bundled helper: runs documentation refresh, optional targeted test pass, full test suite, R CMD build, non-duplicating R CMD check ---manual, pkgdown reference-index validation one command. full test_local() suite already ran, helper skips tests inside R CMD check unless --check-tests supplied. also defaults _R_CHECK_FORCE_SUGGESTS_=false local developer machines every optional backend installed. faster edit loop focused change, run: quick mode runs targeted test filter, builds source tarball, runs structural package check tests, examples, vignette checks disabled. Use standard command pushing release-scale changes. Use --help see --skip-* check-tuning options.
code: devtools::document() testthat::test_local(stop_on_failure = TRUE) R CMD build . R CMD check --no-manual Shennong_*.tar.gz Rscript scripts/check-prepush.R --filter="utils|de_enrich" Rscript scripts/check-prepush.R --filter="utils|de_enrich" --quick

title: Contributing to Shennong
text: Edit roxygen comments R/ regenerate man/ devtools::document(). Edit README.Rmd, rebuild README.md. Keep vignette chunks safe package checks. Heavy networked workflows must guarded. add new exported function, update _pkgdown.yml change set reference index remains complete.

title: Contributing to Shennong
text: Add tests public behavior touch. Prefer lightweight fixtures synthetic matrices network downloads. Skip optional-package tests skip_if_not_installed() appropriate.

title: Contributing to Shennong
text: repository uses Conventional Commits. Preferred format: Examples: feat(clustering): consolidate single harmony workflows fix(io): preserve row names metadata import docs(readme): expand quick-start examples test(preprocessing): cover qc filtering helpers Recommended types: feat fix refactor docs test build ci chore Breaking changes use ! documented NEWS.md.

title: Differential Abundance and State Priority
text: Use permutation backend transparent validation Milo neighborhood-level effects: Bayesian compositional inference, run scCODA pertpy Python environment owned project pass effect table explicit adapter. table needs feature cell_type column effect estimate column; inclusion probabilities credible-effect flags retained supplied. adapter convert posterior inclusion probabilities frequentist p values; fields remain missing unless backend explicitly provides . Discover retrieve durable result:
code: library(Shennong)  object <- sn_test_abundance(   object,   method = "propeller",   sample_by = "patient",   condition_by = "response",   cell_type_by = "cell_type",   contrast = c("responder", "nonresponder"),   design = c("batch", "sex"),   store_name = "response_abundance" ) permutation <- sn_test_abundance(   object, method = "permutation", sample_by = "patient",   condition_by = "response", cell_type_by = "cell_type",   contrast = c("responder", "nonresponder"), permutations = 999,   return_object = FALSE )  milo <- sn_test_abundance(   object, method = "milo", sample_by = "patient",   condition_by = "response", cell_type_by = "cell_type",   contrast = c("responder", "nonresponder"),   backend_control = list(milo = list(reduction = "pca")),   return_object = FALSE ) sccoda <- sn_test_abundance(   object, method = "sccoda", sample_by = "patient",   condition_by = "response", cell_type_by = "cell_type",   contrast = c("responder", "nonresponder"),   backend_control = list(result = list(table = sccoda_effects)),   return_object = FALSE ) sccoda$tables$primary sn_list_results(object, type = "differential_abundance") abundance <- sn_get_result(   object, "differential_abundance", "response_abundance" ) abundance$tables$primary abundance$tables$sample_proportions abundance$tables$sample_contributions sn_plot_abundance(abundance)

title: Differential Abundance and State Priority
text: default method holds biological samples sample_by supplied, predicts condition independently within state, records sample-label permutation null. RareQ first discovers topology-supported rare states tests sample-level abundance association. Scissor answers different question therefore requires gene--bulk-sample expression matrix plus phenotype; cell metadata label accepted substitute.
code: object <- sn_prioritize_states(   object,   method = "augur",   phenotype = "response",   sample_by = "patient",   state_by = "cell_type",   contrast = c("responder", "nonresponder"),   store_name = "response_priority" )  priority <- sn_get_result(object, "state_priority", "response_priority") priority$tables$primary priority$tables$sample_contributions priority$tables$permutation_null sn_plot_state_priority(priority) rareq <- sn_prioritize_states(   object, method = "rareq", phenotype = "response",   sample_by = "patient", contrast = c("responder", "nonresponder"),   return_object = FALSE )  scissor <- sn_prioritize_states(   object, method = "scissor", phenotype = "bulk_response",   state_by = "cell_type", sample_by = "patient",   bulk_expression = bulk_expression,   bulk_phenotype = bulk_response,   family = "binomial",   return_object = FALSE )

what: Discover methods before running a workflow
title: Analysis methods and stored-result contracts
text: Use implemented distinguish shipped Shennong adapter roadmap entry. Use available determine whether optional R package, executable, pixi runtime present current environment. Installation always explicit; discovery never installs software.

BMC Genomics 2018"

title: Analysis methods and stored-result contracts
text: New workflows store results one versioned contract. Tables, embeddings, graphs, models, diagnostics, warnings, parameters, input summaries, provenance stable locations.
code: result <- list(   schema_version = "1.0",   analysis_type = "trajectory",   name = "cd8_slingshot",   method = "slingshot",   backend = "slingshot",   input = list(reduction = "pca", cells = 4L, features = 2L),   parameters = list(start = "naive"),   tables = list(     primary = data.frame(       cell = paste0("cell", 1:4),       pseudotime = c(0, 0.3, 0.7, 1)     )   ),   embeddings = list(),   graphs = list(),   models = list(),   diagnostics = list(converged = TRUE),   warnings = character(),   provenance = list(     package_versions = list(Shennong = as.character(packageVersion("Shennong"))),     random_seed = 1L,     timestamp = "2026-07-15 UTC"   ) )  sn_validate_result(result, error = FALSE) #> $valid #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $errors #> character(0) #>  #> $warnings #> character(0) #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "sn_result_validation" "list"

what: Store, discover, and retrieve from Seurat
title: Analysis methods and stored-result contracts
text: generic functions support new result types existing helpers sn_get_de_result() remain available. Registered legacy result writers upgraded contract write read. Prefer APIs direct object@misc access. Stable names explicit provenance make stored results discoverable scripts, reports, future agent integrations without allowing LLM overwrite computational output.
code: object <- sn_store_result(   object,   type = "trajectory",   name = "cd8_slingshot",   result = result )  sn_list_results(object) sn_list_results(object, type = "trajectory")  trajectory <- sn_get_result(   object,   type = "trajectory",   name = "cd8_slingshot" )  trajectory$tables$primary trajectory$provenance  object <- sn_delete_result(   object,   type = "trajectory",   name = "cd8_slingshot" )

what: Give agents a read-only discovery surface
title: Analysis methods and stored-result contracts
text: Install packaged Shennong Agent Skills register bundled stdio MCP server MCP-capable client: MCP server exposes list_methods, method_status, function_help, workflow_guide, package_info. tools read installed package metadata documentation. Analysis execution remains explicit R code, server offers arbitrary-code file-mutation tool.
code: sn_install_codex_skill(   path = "~/.agents/skills",   type = "package_skills" )  sn_mcp_server_config() #> $command #> [1] "/opt/R/4.6.1/lib/R/bin/Rscript" #>  #> $args #> [1] "-e"                        "Shennong::sn_mcp_server()" #>  #> $transport #> [1] "stdio"

title: Markers, signatures, pathways, and annotation
text: result stored object@misc$de_results. Retrieve name instead relying temporary variable earlier script.
code: library(Shennong) library(Seurat) library(dplyr)  pbmc <- sn_load_data("pbmc3k") #> INFO [2026-07-20 05:54:55] Initializing Seurat object for project: pbmc3k. #> INFO [2026-07-20 05:54:55] Running QC metrics for human. #> INFO [2026-07-20 05:54:56] Seurat object initialization complete.  pbmc <- sn_run_cluster(   object = pbmc,   normalization_method = "seurat",   nfeatures = 1500,   dims = 1:15,   resolution = 0.6,   species = "human",   verbose = FALSE )  pbmc <- sn_find_de(   object = pbmc,   analysis = "markers",   group_by = "seurat_clusters",   layer = "data",   min_pct = 0.25,   logfc_threshold = 0.25,   store_name = "cluster_markers",   return_object = TRUE,   verbose = FALSE ) marker_tbl <- sn_get_de_result(   pbmc,   de_name = "cluster_markers",   top_n = 5 )  head(marker_tbl) #> # A tibble: 6 × 7 #>       p_val avg_log2FC pct.1 pct.2 p_val_adj cluster gene   #>       <dbl>      <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>     <dbl> <fct>   <chr>  #> 1 6.44e- 89       2.52 0.42  0.083  3.54e-84 0       AQP3   #> 2 2.85e- 60       2.49 0.278 0.049  1.56e-55 0       CD40LG #> 3 7.42e- 59       2.38 0.295 0.06   4.07e-54 0       LMNA   #> 4 1.08e- 48       1.81 0.371 0.116  5.93e-44 0       TRADD  #> 5 1.28e- 54       1.78 0.381 0.105  7.01e-50 0       TRAT1  #> 6 4.22e-102       2.49 0.512 0.118  2.31e-97 1       CCR7 names(pbmc@misc$de_results) #> [1] "cluster_markers"

title: Markers, signatures, pathways, and annotation
text: Marker tables often contain hundreds significant genes. Use sn_annotate_de_features() flag marker genes especially useful mechanistic interpretation validation, transcription factors, surface plasma-membrane genes, cytokines, chemokines. called Seurat object, annotated table stored another DE result, can discovered retrieved result helpers.

what: Plot stored markers without hand-copying gene lists
title: Markers, signatures, pathways, and annotation
text: DE result stored, sn_plot_dot() can select top markers per cluster directly.  canonical checks, pass marker genes explicitly.
code: sn_plot_dot(   x = pbmc,   features = "top_markers",   de_name = "cluster_markers",   n = 4,   group_by = "seurat_clusters",   palette = "RdBu",   title = "Top PBMC3k markers" ) sn_plot_dot(   x = pbmc,   features = c("IL7R", "CCR7", "MS4A1", "CD79A", "LYZ", "S100A8", "NKG7"),   group_by = "seurat_clusters",   palette = "Purples",   direction = 1,   title = "Canonical PBMC markers" )

title: Markers, signatures, pathways, and annotation
text: sn_run_annotation() stable annotation entry point. default method = "consensus" scores bundled canonical marker database, retains best second-best candidate, calibrates confidence margin, assigns hierarchical labels, maps known labels versioned Cell Ontology snapshot. supports cluster- cell-level output; without reference, cell inherits traceable consensus cluster. Every final label remains linked marker support, conflicts, reference coverage, confidence, provenance. LLM helpers can explain evidence replace stored computational label. Review uncertain assignments using biological truth.   trusted labels present, confusion heatmap makes disagreements visible.
code: pbmc <- sn_run_annotation(   pbmc,   group_by = "seurat_clusters",   method = "consensus",   species = "human",   tissue = "peripheral blood",   ontology = TRUE,   store_name = "pbmc_consensus" )  annotation_index <- sn_list_results(pbmc, type = "annotation") annotation_index #> # title: Markers, signatures, pathways, and annotation
text: Use sn_list_methods("annotation") sn_method_status() inspect current availability. Reference methods used tissue, disease, species, assay chemistry, cell-state coverage badly mismatched; high similarity incomplete reference evidence missing cell type absent. Backend-specific functions stay internal rather creating parallel result types. Select backend sn_run_annotation(method = ...) retrieve every result sn_get_result(object, "annotation", name) contract. underlying methods cited publications: SingleR (Aran et al., 2019), CellTypist (Dominguez Conde et al., 2022), Seurat mapping (Hao et al., 2021), Symphony (Kang et al., 2021), scmap (Kiselev et al., 2018), scANVI (Xu et al., 2021). Cell Ontology identifiers come bundled 2026-06-08 CL snapshot; verify current author ontology guidelines preparing submission.
code: pbmc <- sn_run_annotation(   pbmc,   group_by = "seurat_clusters",   method = "consensus",   reference = reference,   reference_label_by = "cell_type",   consensus_reference_method = "singleR",   species = "human",   store_name = "pbmc_reference_consensus" )  reference_result <- sn_get_result(   pbmc,   type = "annotation",   name = "pbmc_reference_consensus" )  head(reference_result$tables$backend_predictions) head(reference_result$tables$backend_evidence)

title: Markers, signatures, pathways, and annotation
text: Marker tables useful want name clusters manually. trusted reference already exists, sn_transfer_labels() follows Seurat’s anchor workflow writes projected label plus confidence score back query metadata. wrapper keeps source label transfer settings query@misc$label_transfer, annotation just loose metadata column. query-first interface can use Coralysis reference mapping reference trained sn_run_cluster(integration_method = "coralysis"). default, Shennong keeps native Coralysis-trained SingleCellExperiment PCA model reference@misc$coralysis, returned object can used directly label-transfer reference. durable handoffs, prepare compact transfer-ready reference instead saving full analysis object. Coralysis references keep trained models, PCA model, feature names, selected labels dropping large reference assay matrices joint-probability tables.
code: reference <- pbmc query <- pbmc  reference$cell_type <- reference$seurat_clusters  query <- sn_transfer_labels(   object = query,   reference = reference,   label_by = "cell_type",   prediction_prefix = "pbmc_reference",   dims = 1:15,   verbose = FALSE )  table(query$pbmc_reference_label) head(query[[]][, c("pbmc_reference_label", "pbmc_reference_score")]) reference <- sn_run_cluster(   reference,   batch = "sample_id",   integration_method = "coralysis",   normalization_method = "seurat",   verbose = FALSE )  query <- sn_transfer_labels(   object = query,   reference = reference,   label_by = "cell_type",   method = "coralysis",   prediction_prefix = "coral_reference",   transfer_control = list(k.nn = 10),   verbose = FALSE ) coral_reference <- sn_prepare_label_transfer_reference(   reference,   label_by = "cell_type",   method = "coralysis",   path = "data/processed/pbmc_coralysis_reference.qs2",   overwrite = TRUE )  query <- sn_transfer_labels(   object = query,   reference = coral_reference,   label_by = "cell_type",   method = "coralysis",   prediction_prefix = "coral_reference",   verbose = FALSE )

title: Markers, signatures, pathways, and annotation
text: Shennong ships signature catalog blocking genes, marker sets, report features need redefined every analysis. Custom signatures can added, renamed, deleted API. Use project-specific catalog path want modify package-level catalog.
code: signature_index <- sn_list_signatures(species = "human") head(signature_index) #> # A tibble: 6 × 5 #>   species path                   name          kind      n_genes #>   <chr>   <chr>                  <chr>         <chr>       <int> #> 1 human   Blocklists/Pseudogenes Pseudogenes   signature   12600 #> 2 human   Blocklists/Non-coding  Non-coding    signature    7783 #> 3 human   Programs/HeatShock     HeatShock     signature      97 #> 4 human   Programs/cellCycle.G1S cellCycle.G1S signature      42 #> 5 human   Programs/cellCycle.G2M cellCycle.G2M signature      52 #> 6 human   Programs/IFN           IFN           signature     107  immune_signatures <- sn_get_signatures(   species = "human",   category = c("mito", "ribo") )  head(immune_signatures) #> [1] "MT-ATP6" "MT-ATP8" "MT-CO1"  "MT-CO2"  "MT-CO3"  "MT-CYB" catalog_path <- "config/signatures/pbmc_signatures.csv"  sn_add_signature(   species = "human",   path = "custom/t_cell_activation",   genes = c("IL7R", "CCR7", "LTB"),   catalog_path = catalog_path,   source = "project" )  sn_update_signature(   species = "human",   path = "custom/t_cell_activation",   rename_to = "custom/naive_t_cell",   catalog_path = catalog_path )  sn_delete_signature(   species = "human",   path = "custom/naive_t_cell",   catalog_path = catalog_path )

what: Score programs in cells and samples
title: Markers, signatures, pathways, and annotation
text: sn_score_programs() accepts named gene-set lists, program/gene tables, named gene vectors, bundled signature queries. UCell default per-cell work rank-based score works directly sparse expression less sensitive dataset composition raw mean. AUCell useful area--recovery-curve activity intended statistic, ranking step expensive. GSVA ssGSEA intended primarily samples aggregated groups; supply group_by large single-cell objects. mean backend dependency-free transparent, sensitive assay scale signature size. UCell AUCell require optional Bioconductor packages run CPU. GSVA/ssGSEA require GSVA run CPU; Shennong blocks per-cell GSVA 5,000 sparse columns unless users aggregate explicitly. Cite UCell (Andreatta Carmona, 2021), AUCell (Aibar et al., 2017), GSVA (Hänzelmann et al., 2013), ssGSEA (Barbie et al., 2009) according selected backend.   comparative studies, preserve sample patient inferential unit. sn_test_programs() first averages cell scores within sample/condition/stratum, runs selected test; omitting sample_by explicitly marked exploratory cell-level inference.
code: immune_programs <- list(   B_cell = c("MS4A1", "CD79A", "CD37", "CD74"),   T_cell = c("CD3D", "CD3E", "TRAC", "LTB"),   Myeloid = c("LYZ", "FCN1", "S100A8", "CTSS") )  pbmc <- sn_score_programs(   pbmc,   signatures = immune_programs,   method = "ucell",   assay = "RNA",   layer = "data",   name = "immune_programs",   min_genes = 2 )  program_result <- sn_get_result(   pbmc,   type = "program_scoring",   name = "immune_programs" )  program_result$tables$coverage #> # A tibble: 3 × 6 #>   program n_genes n_matched coverage matched_genes        missing_genes #>   <chr>     <int>     <int>    <dbl> <chr>                <chr>         #> 1 B_cell        4         4        1 MS4A1;CD79A;CD37;CD… ""            #> 2 T_cell        4         4        1 CD3D;CD3E;TRAC;LTB   ""            #> 3 Myeloid       4         4        1 LYZ;FCN1;S100A8;CTSS "" head(program_result$tables$scores) #> # A tibble: 6 × 5 #>   entity           program score level group_by #>   <chr>            <chr>   <dbl> <chr> <chr>    #> 1 AAACATACAACCAC-1 B_cell  0.352 cell  NA       #> 2 AAACATTGAGCTAC-1 B_cell  0.913 cell  NA       #> 3 AAACATTGATCAGC-1 B_cell  0.177 cell  NA       #> 4 AAACCGTGCTTCCG-1 B_cell  0.464 cell  NA       #> 5 AAACCGTGTATGCG-1 B_cell  0.181 cell  NA       #> 6 AAACGCACTGGTAC-1 B_cell  0     cell  NA sn_plot_program_activity(   pbmc,   name = "immune_programs",   group_by = "seurat_clusters" ) sn_plot_program_heatmap(   pbmc,   name = "immune_programs",   group_by = "seurat_clusters" ) pbmc <- sn_test_programs(   pbmc,   score_name = "immune_programs",   condition_by = "condition",   sample_by = "patient",   group_by = "cell_type",   contrast = c("treated", "control"),   method = "limma",   store_name = "immune_program_condition" )  sn_get_result(   pbmc,   type = "program_comparison",   name = "immune_program_condition" )$tables$primary

what: Run enrichment from stored marker results
title: Markers, signatures, pathways, and annotation
text: sn_enrich() can accept gene vector, ranked vector, data frame, Seurat object stored DE results. cluster markers, Seurat-object path reproducible enrichment knows DE result came . already enrichment table another tool, store sn_store_enrichment() interpretation layer can find .
code: pbmc <- sn_enrich(   x = pbmc,   source_de_name = "cluster_markers",   species = "human",   database = "GOBP",   store_name = "cluster_gobp",   return_object = TRUE ) #> INFO [2026-07-20 05:56:23] Running ORA analysis for the GOBP database.  pathways <- sn_get_enrichment_result(   pbmc,   enrichment_name = "cluster_gobp",   top_n = 5 )  head(pathways) #> # A tibble: 5 × 12 #>   ID     Description GeneRatio BgRatio RichFactor FoldEnrichment zScore #>   <chr>  <chr>       <chr>     <chr>        <dbl>          <dbl>  <dbl> #> 1 GO:00… regulation… 159/2481  453/18…      0.351           2.67   14.0 #> 2 GO:19… mononuclea… 152/2481  439/18…      0.346           2.63   13.5 #> 3 GO:00… generation… 147/2481  438/18…      0.336           2.55   12.8 #> 4 GO:00… regulation… 146/2481  472/18…      0.309           2.35   11.6 #> 5 GO:00… nucleotide… 146/2481  478/18…      0.305           2.32   11.4 #> # ℹ 5 more variables: pvalue <dbl>, p.adjust <dbl>, qvalue <dbl>, #> #   geneID <chr>, Count <int> external_terms <- data.frame(   cluster = "0",   ID = "GO:0006955",   Description = "immune response",   p.adjust = 0.001,   geneID = "IL7R/CCR7/LTB" )  pbmc <- sn_store_enrichment(   object = pbmc,   result = external_terms,   store_name = "external_gobp",   analysis = "ora",   database = "GOBP",   species = "human",   source_de_name = "cluster_markers",   return_object = TRUE )

title: Markers, signatures, pathways, and annotation
text: Cell communication run real backend rather ad hoc ligand-receptor table. sn_run_cell_communication() keeps stored-result pattern used DE enrichment. Use CellChat global interaction network, NicheNet question sender--receiver ligand activity, LIANA optional LIANA package available consensus scoring preferred. NicheNet needs ligand-target matrix ligand-receptor network. Shennong requires priors explicitly output remains traceable real NicheNet model rather inferred placeholder network. transcription-factor pathway activity, sn_run_regulatory_activity() uses fast footprint methods decoupleR. DoRothEA reports TF activity; PROGENy reports pathway activity.
code: pbmc <- sn_run_cell_communication(   object = pbmc,   method = "cellchat",   group_by = "seurat_clusters",   species = "human",   store_name = "cluster_cellchat" )  cellchat_tbl <- sn_get_cell_communication_result(   pbmc,   communication_name = "cluster_cellchat" )  head(cellchat_tbl) pbmc <- sn_run_cell_communication(   object = pbmc,   method = "nichenetr",   group_by = "seurat_clusters",   sender = c("0", "1"),   receiver = "2",   geneset = c("IL7R", "CCR7", "LTB"),   ligand_target_matrix = ligand_target_matrix,   lr_network = lr_network,   store_name = "sender_receiver_nichenet" ) pbmc <- sn_run_regulatory_activity(   object = pbmc,   method = "dorothea",   group_by = "seurat_clusters",   species = "human",   store_name = "cluster_dorothea" )  tf_activity <- sn_get_regulatory_activity_result(   pbmc,   activity_name = "cluster_dorothea",   sources = c("NFKB1", "STAT1") )  head(tf_activity)

title: Markers, signatures, pathways, and annotation
text: CellTypist Python command-line tool, Shennong keeps explicit. input Seurat object, predictions written back metadata. input file path, Shennong returns prediction table object update. intended pattern evidence first, label second: markers pathways computed stored automated LLM-assisted annotation consumes .
code: pbmc <- sn_run_celltypist(   x = pbmc,   model = "Immune_All_Low.pkl",   over_clustering = "seurat_clusters",   majority_voting = TRUE,   quiet = TRUE )  grep("Immune_All_Low", colnames(pbmc[[]]), value = TRUE) prediction_tbl <- sn_run_celltypist(   x = "pbmc3k_counts.csv",   model = "Immune_All_Low.pkl",   over_clustering = "obs_cluster",   quiet = TRUE )  head(prediction_tbl)

what: Build a small reference and mock bulk matrix
title: Bulk deconvolution from a PBMC3k reference
text: mock bulk samples sums selected single-cell clusters. Real bulk data normalized gene-aligned according backend requirements.
code: library(Shennong) library(Seurat) library(dplyr)  pbmc <- sn_load_data("pbmc3k") #> INFO [2026-07-20 05:57:03] Initializing Seurat object for project: pbmc3k. #> INFO [2026-07-20 05:57:04] Running QC metrics for human. #> INFO [2026-07-20 05:57:04] Seurat object initialization complete.  pbmc <- sn_run_cluster(   object = pbmc,   normalization_method = "seurat",   nfeatures = 1500,   dims = 1:15,   resolution = 0.6,   species = "human",   verbose = FALSE )  pbmc$cell_type <- paste0("cluster_", pbmc$seurat_clusters)  counts <- SeuratObject::LayerData(pbmc, assay = "RNA", layer = "counts")  bulk <- cbind(   bulk_a = Matrix::rowSums(counts[, pbmc$cell_type %in% c("cluster_0", "cluster_1"), drop = FALSE]),   bulk_b = Matrix::rowSums(counts[, pbmc$cell_type %in% c("cluster_2", "cluster_3"), drop = FALSE]) )

what: Prepare a CIBERSORTx run without launching the container
title: Bulk deconvolution from a PBMC3k reference
text: Use cibersortx_dry_run = TRUE validate exported files command bundle locally. right mode examples, tests, documentation. actual CIBERSORTx run, store credentials locally omit placeholder values.
code: bundle <- sn_deconvolve_bulk(   x = pbmc,   bulk = bulk,   method = "cibersortx",   cell_type_by = "cell_type",   outdir = file.path(tempdir(), "pbmc3k-cibersortx"),   prefix = "pbmc3k_demo",   cibersortx_email = "demo@example.org",   cibersortx_token = "fake-token",   cibersortx_dry_run = TRUE,   return_object = FALSE )  names(bundle) #> [1] "table"     "files"     "artifacts" "method" bundle$command #> NULL bundle$files #> $single_cell_reference #> [1] "/tmp/RtmpCDQDhC/pbmc3k-cibersortx/sample_file_for_cibersort.txt" #>  #> $mixture #> [1] "/tmp/RtmpCDQDhC/pbmc3k-cibersortx/mixture_file_for_cibersort.txt" #>  #> $signature_matrix #> [1] "/tmp/RtmpCDQDhC/pbmc3k-cibersortx/CIBERSORTx_sample_file_for_cibersort_inferred_phenoclasses.CIBERSORTx_sample_file_for_cibersort_inferred_refsample.bm.K999.txt" #>  #> $result #> [1] "/tmp/RtmpCDQDhC/pbmc3k-cibersortx/CIBERSORTx_pbmc3k_demo_Results.txt" sn_set_cibersortx_credentials(   email = Sys.getenv("CIBERSORTX_EMAIL"),   token = Sys.getenv("CIBERSORTX_TOKEN") )  result <- sn_deconvolve_bulk(   x = pbmc,   bulk = bulk,   method = "cibersortx",   cell_type_by = "cell_type",   outdir = "results/deconvolution/cibersortx",   prefix = "pbmc3k" )

title: Bulk deconvolution from a PBMC3k reference
text: CIBERSORTx already produced fraction table, import cibersortx_result store table explicitly. Keeping results reference object makes downstream retrieval predictable.
code: fractions <- data.frame(   sample = c("bulk_a", "bulk_b"),   cluster_0 = c(0.45, 0.10),   cluster_1 = c(0.35, 0.05),   cluster_2 = c(0.10, 0.50),   cluster_3 = c(0.10, 0.35),   check.names = FALSE )  pbmc <- sn_store_deconvolution(   object = pbmc,   result = fractions,   store_name = "pbmc3k_mock_bulk",   method = "cibersortx",   bulk_samples = fractions$sample,   reference_label = "cell_type",   artifacts = bundle$files,   return_object = TRUE )  sn_get_deconvolution_result(   pbmc,   deconvolution_name = "pbmc3k_mock_bulk" ) #> # A tibble: 2 × 5 #>   sample cluster_0 cluster_1 cluster_2 cluster_3 #>   <chr>      <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1 bulk_a      0.45      0.35       0.1      0.1  #> 2 bulk_b      0.1       0.05       0.5      0.35

what: BayesPrism is a local optional backend
title: Bulk deconvolution from a PBMC3k reference
text: BayesPrism runs inside R useful want Bayesian local workflow. optional much heavier dry-run CIBERSORTx export. rule thumb : use dry-run mode verify file contracts, run backend deliberately environment dependencies credentials configured.
code: bayesprism_result <- sn_deconvolve_bulk(   x = pbmc,   bulk = bulk,   method = "bayesprism",   cell_type_by = "cell_type",   key = NULL,   n_cores = 2,   store_name = "pbmc3k_bayesprism" )

title: Bulk transcriptomics from QC to clinical association
text: sn_assess_bulk_qc() retains sample metrics, expression quantiles, PCA, correlation, robust outlier flags one validated result.
code: qc <- sn_assess_bulk_qc(counts, sample_data) qc$tables$samples[, c("sample", "library_size", "outlier")] #> # A tibble: 8 × 3 #>   sample   library_size outlier #>   <chr>           <dbl> <lgl>   #> 1 sample_1         2404 FALSE   #> 2 sample_2         2433 FALSE   #> 3 sample_3         2405 FALSE   #> 4 sample_4         2309 FALSE   #> 5 sample_5         2733 FALSE   #> 6 sample_6         2741 FALSE   #> 7 sample_7         2734 FALSE   #> 8 sample_8         2553 FALSE sn_plot_bulk_pca(qc, sample_data, color_by = "condition")

title: Bulk transcriptomics from QC to clinical association
text: contrast contract always c(variable, numerator, denominator). integer counts, method = "auto" chooses edgeR; normalized continuous input chooses limma; mixed-effects formula selects dream. Explicit DESeq2, edgeR, limma, dream choices remain available. sn_find_de() detects Seurat input single-cell matrix/list/ SummarizedExperiment input bulk. Set modality = "single_cell" modality = "bulk" dispatch must explicit. older sn_find_bulk_de() entry point remains compatibility wrapper around bulk implementation.
code: de <- sn_find_de(   counts,   metadata = sample_data,   design = ~ batch + condition,   contrast = c("condition", "tumor", "normal"),   method = "auto" )  # Discover and retrieve the standardized evidence table. names(de$tables) head(de$tables$differential_expression) sn_plot_bulk_de(de)

title: Bulk transcriptomics from QC to clinical association
text: Pathway scoring reports gene-set coverage returning sample scores. WGCNA returns modules, eigengenes, power diagnostics, module-trait associations. Cox models general clinical associations can use either genes expression matrix numeric columns sample metadata. sn_run_bulk() dispatches workflows workflow = "qc", "de", "pathway", "network", "survival". explicit functions preferable scripts required inputs easier review.
code: pathways <- sn_score_bulk_pathways(   counts,   signatures = list(inflammation = paste0("gene_", 1:10)),   metadata = sample_data ) pathways$tables$coverage pathways$tables$scores  network <- sn_run_wgcna(counts, sample_data, traits = "condition") network$tables$modules network$tables$trait_associations  survival <- sn_run_survival(   counts, time = "time", event = "event",   features = c("gene_1", "gene_2"), metadata = sample_data ) survival$tables$survival sn_plot_survival(survival)

what: One function for the standard clustering path
title: Clustering and integration with PBMC3k
text: minimal clustering call intentionally short. arguments usually matter report visible: normalization method, number features, PC range, resolution, clustering algorithm, species. input already contains sn_run_cluster() stage metadata, function reuses matching stages default. makes resolution tuning cheap: sn_run_cluster(pbmc, resolution = 1.0) reuses normalization, HVGs, PCA, neighbor graph, UMAP, recomputes cluster labels. Use rerun_from = "integration" changing integration backend, reuse = FALSE completely fresh run needed. UMAP geometry can tuned independently clustering umap_control. useful clusters biologically separate overlap visually two-dimensional embedding. result regular Seurat object. can use Shennong plotting helpers drop Seurat whenever needed.
code: library(Shennong) library(Seurat) library(dplyr)  pbmc <- sn_load_data("pbmc3k") #> INFO [2026-07-20 05:58:30] Initializing Seurat object for project: pbmc3k. #> INFO [2026-07-20 05:58:30] Running QC metrics for human. #> INFO [2026-07-20 05:58:30] Seurat object initialization complete.  pbmc <- sn_run_cluster(   object = pbmc,   normalization_method = "seurat",   nfeatures = 2000,   npcs = 30,   dims = 1:20,   resolution = 0.6,   cluster_algorithm = "leiden",   species = "human",   verbose = FALSE )  pbmc #> An object of class Seurat  #> 54872 features across 2753 samples within 1 assay  #> Active assay: RNA (54872 features, 2000 variable features) #>  3 layers present: counts, data, scale.data #>  2 dimensional reductions calculated: pca, umap pbmc_umap <- sn_run_cluster(   pbmc,   umap_control = list(     n.neighbors = 20,     min.dist = 0.1,     spread = 1,     reduction.name = "umap_tuned"   ),   rerun_from = "umap" ) sn_plot_dim(   object = pbmc,   reduction = "umap",   group_by = "seurat_clusters",   label = TRUE,   title = "PBMC3k clusters" )

title: Clustering and integration with PBMC3k
text: Single-cell clustering can dominated cell-cycle, mitochondrial, ribosomal, immunoglobulin, pseudogene, heat-shock genes. block_genes makes choice explicit instead hiding inside custom variable-feature script. vector can mix bundled signature names, full signature paths, custom gene symbols, applied internally selected HVGs log-normalization SCTransform workflows. Use technical lineage-specific gene family overwhelming structure want resolve.
code: pbmc_blocked <- sn_run_cluster(   object = pbmc,   normalization_method = "seurat",   nfeatures = 2000,   dims = 1:20,   resolution = 0.6,   species = "human",   block_genes = c("cellCycle.G2M", "cellCycle.G1S", "mito", "ribo", "MYC"),   verbose = FALSE )

title: Clustering and integration with PBMC3k
text: Small populations can disappear variable genes selected global variance. Shennong can append rare-aware features PCA. key idea simple: keep global HVGs, add small number genes help preserve rare groups. rare-cell scoring helper can used independently inspect cells look unusual.
code: pbmc_rare <- sn_run_cluster(   object = pbmc,   normalization_method = "seurat",   nfeatures = 1500,   rare_feature_method = c("gini", "local_markers"),   rare_feature_group_by = "seurat_clusters",   rare_feature_n = 50,   rare_feature_control = list(group_max_fraction = 0.05),   dims = 1:20,   resolution = 0.8,   species = "human",   verbose = FALSE ) rare_tbl <- sn_detect_rare_cells(   pbmc,   method = "gini",   nfeatures = 200 )  head(rare_tbl[order(rare_tbl$rare_score, decreasing = TRUE), ]) #>               cell_id method rare_score rare_cell #> 1605 GACTGAACCCTGAA-1   gini   43.13258      TRUE #> 283  ACGAACTGGCTATG-1   gini   31.47734      TRUE #> 432  AGAGGTCTACAGCT-1   gini   26.91956      TRUE #> 256  ACCCACTGGTTCAG-1   gini   25.88071      TRUE #> 1360 CTAGGATGAGCCTA-1   gini   25.18387      TRUE #> 35   AAATGGGAAGGCGA-1   gini   20.34990      TRUE

what: Force known marker genes into the PCA feature set
title: Clustering and integration with PBMC3k
text: Sometimes already know marker genes separate rare population, genes sparse rank among global HVGs. Use hvg_features merge genes final ScaleData/PCA feature set Shennong validates exist object. different rare_feature_method: rare_feature_method asks Shennong discover rare-aware features, hvg_features records user-provided feature prior.
code: pbmc_manual <- sn_run_cluster(   object = pbmc,   normalization_method = "seurat",   nfeatures = 1500,   hvg_features = c("IL7R", "CCR7", "NKG7", "MS4A1"),   dims = 1:20,   resolution = 0.6,   species = "human",   verbose = FALSE )  pbmc_manual@misc$hvg_selection$user_features #> [1] "IL7R"  "CCR7"  "NKG7"  "MS4A1"

title: Clustering and integration with PBMC3k
text: CITE-seq objects paired RNA ADT assays, set modality = "cite_seq". default multimodal_method = "wnn" runs standard RNA PCA path, normalizes ADT assay CLR, computes ADT PCA, uses Seurat weighted nearest neighbors cluster wsnn graph create wnn.umap embedding. template assumes pbmc_cite prepared Seurat object containing assays, executed site builds. sn_run_multimodal(object, method = "wnn") equivalent explicit entry point accepts remaining sn_run_cluster() arguments .... batch-aware CITE-seq integration, choose multimodal backend explicitly. multimodal_method = "totalvi" runs scvi-tools totalVI RNA counts ADT counts. multimodal_method = "coralysis" runs native Coralysis ADT assay log-normalized protein matrix. multimodal_method = "mmochi" runs MMoCHi ADT landmark registration across supplied batch column, single-sample mode batch = NULL. stores corrected protein matrix assay layer local Seurat object supports arbitrary layers otherwise object@misc$mmochi$corrected_protein, computes protein-derived mmochi reduction, clusters reduction. full MMoCHi hierarchy classifier remains advanced Python workflow requires explicit user-defined hierarchy.
code: pbmc_cite <- sn_run_cluster(   object = pbmc_cite,   modality = "cite_seq",   assay = "RNA",   adt_assay = "ADT",   normalization_method = "seurat",   nfeatures = 2000,   dims = 1:20,   adt_dims = 1:18,   resolution = 0.6,   species = "human",   verbose = FALSE )  sn_plot_dim(pbmc_cite, reduction = "wnn.umap", group_by = "seurat_clusters") pbmc_totalvi <- sn_run_cluster(   object = pbmc_cite,   modality = "cite_seq",   multimodal_method = "totalvi",   batch = "library",   assay = "RNA",   adt_assay = "ADT",   normalization_method = "seurat",   nfeatures = 3000,   dims = 1:30,   integration_control = list(max_epochs = 100),   verbose = FALSE )  sn_plot_dim(pbmc_totalvi, reduction = "umap", group_by = "seurat_clusters") pbmc_adt_coral <- sn_run_cluster(   object = pbmc_cite,   modality = "cite_seq",   multimodal_method = "coralysis",   batch = "library",   adt_assay = "ADT",   adt_dims = 1:18,   verbose = FALSE ) pbmc_adt_mmochi <- sn_run_cluster(   object = pbmc_cite,   modality = "cite_seq",   multimodal_method = "mmochi",   batch = NULL,   adt_assay = "ADT",   adt_dims = 1:18,   integration_control = list(     protein_layer = "data",     marker_bandwidths = list(CD3 = 0.25),     store_corrected_layer = TRUE   ),   verbose = FALSE )

title: Clustering and integration with PBMC3k
text: Instead treating resolution = 0.8 magic number, sn_sweep_cluster_resolution() reruns clustering small grid reports diagnostics cluster count, silhouette, graph connectivity. recommendation replacement biology; compact way find resolutions numerically stable looking marker genes.
code: sweep <- sn_sweep_cluster_resolution(   pbmc,   resolutions = seq(0.2, 1.0, by = 0.2),   reduction = "pca",   dims = 1:20,   metrics = c("n_clusters", "mean_silhouette", "graph_connectivity"),   max_cells = 1500 )  sweep$summary #>   resolution n_clusters mean_silhouette scaled_silhouette #> 1        0.2          6       0.2971698         0.6485849 #> 2        0.4          9       0.2716079         0.6358039 #> 3        0.6         10       0.2143390         0.6071695 #> 4        0.8         10       0.2100907         0.6050453 #> 5        1.0         11       0.2069939         0.6034970 #>   graph_connectivity scaled_graph_connectivity composite_score #> 1          1.0000000                 1.0000000       0.8242925 #> 2          1.0000000                 1.0000000       0.8179020 #> 3          1.0000000                 1.0000000       0.8035848 #> 4          0.9995556                 0.9995556       0.8023004 #> 5          1.0000000                 1.0000000       0.8017485 sweep$recommended_resolution #> [1] 0.2

what: Integration is the same API with a batch column
title: Clustering and integration with PBMC3k
text: PBMC3k one dataset, example creates teaching-pseudo batch column. real experiment, batch donor, library, chemistry, dataset column. keeps mental model small: single-sample clustering batch integration use entry point; adding batch turns integration, integration_method names backend. Harmony default fast broadly useful. integration switch can used SCTransform normalization: imbalanced datasets rare unevenly distributed state may washed conventional correction, Coralysis can selected explicitly. Coralysis runs log-normalized feature set selected Shennong stores integrated embedding coralysis reduction. trained Coralysis SingleCellExperiment stored default object@misc$coralysis, returned object can used later native Coralysis label-transfer reference. Set integration_control = list(store_sce = FALSE) clustering-runs. Seurat layer integration methods also available users want compare familiar anchor-based workflows: Python integration methods can run pixi-managed scverse project kept user-level Shennong runtime directory, ~/.shennong/. integration_method = "scvi", Shennong exports selected count matrix metadata, renders ~/.shennong/pixi/scvi/pixi.toml package-bundled inst/pixi/scvi/pixi.toml template needed, runs Python backend, imports latent.csv scvi reduction, continues Seurat neighbors, clusters, UMAP. R package vendor Python environments. ships backend runner script pixi config templates; pixi workspaces environment prefixes created ~/.shennong/pixi/, matching package’s ~/.shennong/data/ convention. Use sn_pixi_paths("scvi") inspect layout run. pixi already available, Shennong can install standalone pixi binary sn_ensure_pixi() use Shennong PIXI_HOME ~/.shennong/pixi/home runtime configuration. default accelerator = "auto" uses CUDA pixi environment nvidia-smi reports CUDA-capable GPU otherwise uses CPU environment. China mirror handling can enabled mirror = "auto" specific mirror "tuna". bundled Python environment configs can inspected sn_list_pixi_environments() materialized sn_prepare_pixi_environment(). Method aliases share environments underlying software stack : scANVI uses scvi environment, scPoli uses scarches environment. Spatial tools represented specific environment names cell2location, tangram, squidpy, spatialdata, stlearn. method wrappers define object-level contract, use object = form. Runner scripts stored pixi family inst/pixi/<family>/scripts/. example, inferCNVpy can run directly Seurat object import cell-level CNV metadata CNV reductions back object: Python tools follow Seurat-first pattern. Tools need additional biological inputs make inputs explicit:
code: pbmc$library <- rep(c("library_a", "library_b"), length.out = ncol(pbmc))  pbmc_integrated <- sn_run_cluster(   object = pbmc,   batch = "library",   integration_method = "harmony",   normalization_method = "seurat",   hvg_group_by = "library",   nfeatures = 2000,   dims = 1:20,   resolution = 0.6,   species = "human",   verbose = FALSE )  sn_plot_dim(   object = pbmc_integrated,   reduction = "umap",   group_by = "library",   title = "Pseudo-batch mixing" ) pbmc_sct_integrated <- sn_run_cluster(   object = pbmc,   batch = "library",   integration_method = "harmony",   normalization_method = "sctransform",   hvg_features = c("IL7R", "CCR7", "NKG7"),   dims = 1:20,   resolution = 0.6,   verbose = FALSE ) pbmc_coral <- sn_run_cluster(   object = pbmc,   batch = "library",   integration_method = "coralysis",   normalization_method = "seurat",   hvg_group_by = "library",   nfeatures = 2000,   dims = 1:20,   resolution = 0.6,   integration_control = list(     icp_args = list(L = 25, threads = 2),     pca_args = list(p = 20)   ),   umap_control = list(     n.neighbors = 20,     min.dist = 0.1   ),   verbose = FALSE ) pbmc_cca <- sn_run_cluster(   object = pbmc,   batch = "library",   integration_method = "seurat_cca",   normalization_method = "seurat",   nfeatures = 2000,   dims = 1:20,   verbose = FALSE )  pbmc_rpca <- sn_run_cluster(   object = pbmc,   batch = "library",   integration_method = "seurat_rpca",   normalization_method = "seurat",   nfeatures = 2000,   dims = 1:20,   verbose = FALSE ) tumor <- sn_run_infercnvpy(   object = tumor,   assay = "RNA",   layer = "data",   species = "human",   reference_by = "cell_type",   reference_cat = c("T cell", "Myeloid", "Endothelial") ) object <- sn_run_cellphonedb(object, group_by = "cell_type")  spatial <- sn_run_tangram(   object = spatial,   reference_object = sc_reference,   cell_type_by = "cell_type",   spatial_cols = c("x", "y") )  spatial <- sn_run_cell2location(   object = spatial,   reference_signatures = signature_matrix,   spatial_cols = c("x", "y"),   method_control = list(max_epochs = 30000) )  spatial <- sn_run_squidpy(object = spatial, spatial_cols = c("x", "y")) sn_list_pixi_environments() sn_pixi_paths("scvi") sn_ensure_pixi()  pbmc_scvi <- sn_run_cluster(   object = pbmc,   batch = "library",   integration_method = "scvi",   normalization_method = "seurat",   hvg_group_by = "library",   nfeatures = 3000,   dims = 1:30,   integration_control = list(     accelerator = "auto",     mirror = "auto",     n_latent = 30,     max_epochs = 100   ),   verbose = FALSE )  pbmc_scanvi <- sn_run_cluster(   object = pbmc,   batch = "library",   integration_method = "scanvi",   normalization_method = "seurat",   hvg_group_by = "library",   nfeatures = 3000,   dims = 1:30,   integration_control = list(     label_by = "reference_label",     unlabeled_category = "Unknown",     n_latent = 30,     max_epochs = 100   ),   verbose = FALSE )

title: CNV, Malignancy, and Metabolism
text: Use cell names directly metadata column declare normal references. inferCNVpy default pixi backend. CopyKAT also available run sample sample sample_by supplied. Discover retrieve durable result without reading object@misc directly. primary table contains per-cell CNV, malignancy, call, reference, subclone fields. tables retain chromosome, sample, CNV-expression evidence.
code: tumor <- sn_run_cnv(   tumor,   method = "infercnvpy",   reference_by = "cell_type",   reference_cat = c("T cell", "Myeloid"),   sample_by = "patient",   store_name = "tumor_cnv" ) sn_list_results(tumor, type = "cnv") cnv <- sn_get_result(tumor, "cnv", "tumor_cnv") head(cnv$tables$primary) cnv$tables$sample_summary  sn_plot_cnv(tumor, "tumor_cnv", type = "heatmap") sn_plot_cnv(tumor, "tumor_cnv", type = "umap") sn_plot_cnv(tumor, "tumor_cnv", type = "association")

title: CNV, Malignancy, and Metabolism
text: lightweight default uses curated pathways UCell. GSVA, ssGSEA, sparse-aware mean score alternatives. Always supply sample_by condition comparison; group_by optionally stratifies cell type state. scMetabolism optional R backend. scFEA Compass remain heavyweight external runtimes: pass pathway--cell result explicit runner backend_control, Shennong applies sample-level storage, comparison, plotting contract.
code: sn_metabolic_signatures("human") |> names()  tumor <- sn_run_metabolism(   tumor,   method = "geneset",   scoring_method = "ucell",   sample_by = "patient",   condition_by = "response",   group_by = "cell_type",   contrast = c("responder", "nonresponder"),   store_name = "metabolism_response" )  sn_list_results(tumor, type = "metabolism") metabolism <- sn_get_result(tumor, "metabolism", "metabolism_response") metabolism$tables$coverage metabolism$tables$sample_scores metabolism$tables$differential  sn_plot_metabolism(tumor, "metabolism_response", type = "heatmap") sn_plot_metabolism(tumor, "metabolism_response", type = "differential")

title: Comparable cell-cell communication and consensus
text: Use vector methods comparable backends contribute consensus. Method-specific arguments belong backend_control. NicheNet adds explicit ligand-target links prior network ligand-target matrix supplied. MultiNicheNet requires biological sample condition columns delegates pseudobulk differential analysis official backend.
code: object <- sn_run_cell_communication(   object,   method = c("liana", "cellchat"),   group_by = "cell_type",   sample_by = "patient",   condition_by = "response",   contrast = c("Responder", "Nonresponder"),   consensus = TRUE,   backend_control = list(     liana = list(resource = "consensus"),     cellchat = list(min_cells = 20)   ),   store_name = "tumor_communication" ) object <- sn_run_cell_communication(   object,   method = "multinichenet",   group_by = "cell_type",   sample_by = "patient",   condition_by = "response",   contrast = c("Responder", "Nonresponder"),   sender = c("Myeloid", "Fibroblast"),   receiver = "Tumor",   lr_network = lr_network,   ligand_target_matrix = ligand_target_matrix,   store_name = "differential_communication" )

title: Comparable cell-cell communication and consensus
text: sample-evidence table recomputes ligand receptor expression within biological sample. Condition effects therefore use patients samples replicates. treat cells independent experimental units.
code: sn_list_results(object, type = "cell_communication")  communication <- sn_get_result(   object,   type = "cell_communication",   name = "tumor_communication" )  communication$tables$primary communication$tables$backend_raw communication$tables$consensus communication$tables$method_concordance communication$tables$sample_evidence communication$tables$condition_comparison communication$tables$ligand_targets communication$warnings

title: Comparable cell-cell communication and consensus
code: sn_plot_communication(object, "tumor_communication", type = "bubble") sn_plot_communication(object, "tumor_communication", type = "heatmap") sn_plot_communication(object, "tumor_communication", type = "network") sn_plot_ligand_target(object, "differential_communication") sn_plot_communication_comparison(object, "tumor_communication")

what: Prepare cluster labels and sample metadata
title: Composition and differential abundance
text: pseudo labels demonstration. real analysis, sample biological replicate condition come experimental metadata.
code: library(Shennong) library(Seurat) library(dplyr)  pbmc <- sn_load_data("pbmc3k") #> INFO [2026-07-20 06:04:56] Initializing Seurat object for project: pbmc3k. #> INFO [2026-07-20 06:04:56] Running QC metrics for human. #> INFO [2026-07-20 06:04:57] Seurat object initialization complete.  pbmc <- sn_run_cluster(   object = pbmc,   normalization_method = "seurat",   nfeatures = 1500,   dims = 1:15,   resolution = 0.6,   species = "human",   verbose = FALSE )  pbmc$sample <- rep(paste0("donor", 1:6), length.out = ncol(pbmc)) pbmc$condition <- ifelse(pbmc$sample %in% paste0("donor", 1:3), "control", "stim") pbmc$cluster_label <- paste0("cluster_", pbmc$seurat_clusters)

title: Composition and differential abundance
text: sn_calculate_composition() returns transparent table group totals, category counts, percentages. denominator visible group_total, makes easy audit whether proportion based enough cells. Observed--expected enrichment useful want ask clusters enriched depleted relative full contingency table.
code: composition <- sn_calculate_composition(   x = pbmc,   group_by = "sample",   variable = "cluster_label",   min_cells = 10,   measure = "both" )  head(composition) #> # A tibble: 6 × 5 #>   sample cluster_label count group_total proportion #>   <chr>  <chr>         <int>       <int>      <dbl> #> 1 donor1 cluster_0       109         442      24.7  #> 2 donor1 cluster_1        79         442      17.9  #> 3 donor1 cluster_2        80         442      18.1  #> 4 donor1 cluster_3        59         442      13.3  #> 5 donor1 cluster_4        64         442      14.5  #> 6 donor1 cluster_5        27         442       6.11 roe <- sn_calculate_roe(   x = pbmc,   group_by = "condition",   variable = "cluster_label" )  head(roe) #>   condition cluster_label observed row_total col_total grand_total #> 1   control     cluster_0      317      1377       590        2753 #> 2      stim     cluster_0      273      1376       590        2753 #> 3   control     cluster_3      177      1377       360        2753 #> 4      stim     cluster_3      183      1376       360        2753 #> 5   control     cluster_2      246      1377       479        2753 #> 6      stim     cluster_2      233      1376       479        2753 #>   expected       roe    log2_roe #> 1 295.1072 1.0741861  0.10324394 #> 2 294.8928 0.9257600 -0.11128986 #> 3 180.0654 0.9829763 -0.02477150 #> 4 179.9346 1.0170361  0.02437088 #> 5 239.5870 1.0267669  0.03810871 #> 6 239.4130 0.9732136 -0.03917156 roe_matrix <- sn_calculate_roe(   x = pbmc,   group_by = "condition",   variable = "cluster_label",   return_matrix = TRUE )  roe_matrix[, seq_len(min(4, ncol(roe_matrix))), drop = FALSE] #>         cluster_0 cluster_3 cluster_2 cluster_6 #> control  1.074186 0.9829763 1.0267669 0.8642022 #> stim     0.925760 1.0170361 0.9732136 1.1358965 sn_plot_composition(   composition,   x = sample,   fill = cluster_label,   y = proportion,   position = "stack",   palette = "Paired",   title = "PBMC3k cluster composition by pseudo donor" )

title: Composition and differential abundance
text: sn_compare_composition() summarizes sample first, compares sample-level proportions conditions. avoids pseudo-replication. Use plotting helpers summary table want compact figure-ready view.

title: Composition and differential abundance
text: Composition categorical. continuous per-cell per-sample summaries, summarize replicate level first, plot.
code: sample_qc <- pbmc[[]] |>   dplyr::group_by(sample, condition) |>   dplyr::summarise(     median_features = median(nFeature_RNA),     median_mito = median(percent.mt),     .groups = "drop"   )  sn_plot_boxplot(   sample_qc,   x = condition,   y = median_features,   y_label = "Median detected genes",   title = "QC summary by pseudo condition" )

title: Composition and differential abundance
text: Global proportions can miss local shifts inside embedding. sn_run_milo() wraps miloR neighborhood workflow sample-level differential abundance. milo result generated elsewhere, store retrieve object-level convention used DE enrichment. sn_plot_milo() displays stored neighborhood-level table.  recommended reporting pattern show global composition local neighborhood abundance biological claim depends changes cell-state frequency.
code: milo_tbl <- sn_run_milo(   x = pbmc,   sample_by = "sample",   group_by = "condition",   contrast = c("stim", "control"),   reduction = "pca",   dims = 1:15,   annotation_by = "cluster_label",   max_cells = 1500,   store_name = "condition_da",   return_object = FALSE )  head(milo_tbl) mock_milo <- data.frame(   logFC = c(1.1, -0.7, 0.2),   SpatialFDR = c(0.03, 0.08, 0.4),   cluster_label = c("cluster_0", "cluster_1", "cluster_2") )  pbmc <- sn_store_milo(   object = pbmc,   result = mock_milo,   store_name = "condition_da",   sample_by = "sample",   group_by = "condition",   comparison = "stim_vs_control",   reduction = "pca",   annotation_by = "cluster_label",   return_object = TRUE )  sn_get_milo_result(pbmc, milo_name = "condition_da") #> # A tibble: 3 × 3 #>   logFC SpatialFDR cluster_label #>   <dbl>      <dbl> <chr>         #> 1   1.1       0.03 cluster_0     #> 2  -0.7       0.08 cluster_1     #> 3   0.2       0.4  cluster_2 sn_plot_milo(   pbmc,   milo_name = "condition_da",   annotation_by = "cluster_label",   title = "Neighborhood differential abundance" )

title: Data input, output, and project setup
text: package loader returns Seurat object default. need original 10x H5 path instead, set return_object = FALSE; useful want show exactly read disk. can also request several example datasets ; filtered matrices initialized separately returned one merged Seurat object source dataset stored sample metadata column. sn_load_data() uses general Zenodo downloader underneath. Public Zenodo records need token; pass token = ... restricted private records account can access. current Shennong public reprocessed-data collection indexed shennong_index.json Zenodo. Use sn_list_datasets() discover sample-level IDs, pass one IDs sn_load_data(). study ZIP cached locally, requested sample file extracted.
code: library(Shennong) library(Seurat) library(dplyr)  pbmc <- sn_load_data(dataset = "pbmc3k")  pbmc_h5 <- sn_load_data(   dataset = "pbmc3k",   return_object = FALSE )  pbmc_h5  pbmc_merged <- sn_load_data(dataset = c("pbmc1k", "pbmc3k")) table(pbmc_merged$sample) public_h5 <- sn_download_zenodo(   record_id = "14884845",   files = "pbmc3k_filtered_feature_bc_matrix.h5" )  public_h5 datasets <- sn_list_datasets() head(datasets[, c("dataset", "study_id", "organism", "technology")])  sample_obj <- sn_load_data(dataset = datasets$dataset[[1]]) sample_raw <- sn_load_data(dataset = datasets$dataset[[1]], matrix_type = "raw") sample_metrics <- sn_load_data(dataset = datasets$dataset[[1]], matrix_type = "metrics")

what: Use the Shennong Data Server lazily
title: Data input, output, and project setup
text: resources exposed Shennong Data Server, backend = "api" returns metadata-first ShennongData handle without downloading full matrix. wrapper creates non-default connection server_url, uses resource, assay, layer contracts ShennongData 0.2. Keep backend = "auto" local/Zenodo fallback desired. Use backend = "local" workflow must never contact data server.
code: remote <- sn_load_data(   dataset = "toil",   backend = "api",   server_url = "https://data.example.org",   api_args = list(     assay = "rna",     layer = "expression"   ) )  materialized <- sn_load_data(   dataset = "toil",   backend = "api",   server_url = "https://data.example.org",   lazy = FALSE,   api_args = list(     assay = "rna",     layer = "expression",     collect_args = list(allow_large = TRUE)   ) )

what: Read once, then initialize with metadata
title: Data input, output, and project setup
text: sn_read() handles common tabular files, serialized objects, 10x matrices, H5/H5AD, GMT files, Shennong’s custom dispatchers. 10x H5 matrix, can read counts directly make metadata decision explicit sn_initialize_seurat_object(). important design choice provenance hidden project name. sample_name, study, species separate inputs, downstream composition, integration, interpretation steps can reuse .
code: counts <- sn_read(pbmc_h5)  pbmc <- sn_initialize_seurat_object(   x = counts,   project = "pbmc3k_demo",   sample_name = "pbmc3k",   study = "10x_pbmc",   species = "human" )  pbmc head(pbmc[[]][, c("sample", "study", "nCount_RNA", "nFeature_RNA", "percent.mt")])

title: Data input, output, and project setup
text: Real projects often start several Cell Ranger outputs. sn_list_10x_paths() scans directory tree returns paths Shennong can feed back sn_initialize_seurat_object(). pattern keeps import code short still making biological metadata explicit discovery.
code: tenx_paths <- sn_list_10x_paths(   path = "data/cellranger",   what = "filtered" )  pbmc_samples <- sn_initialize_seurat_object(   x = tenx_paths,   species = "human",   study = "pbmc_atlas" )

title: Data input, output, and project setup
text: Use sn_write() sn_read() together analysis handoffs. API works tables serialized R objects; Shennong chooses writer file extension. row_names = "cell" useful CSV carries cell IDs regular column. avoids ad hoc read.csv(..., row.names = ...) calls easy forget later scripts. large Seurat objects, keep bulky assay layers disk BPCells serializing Seurat object. returned object keeps Seurat layer interface, selected matrices live BPCells directories.
code: outdir <- sn_set_path(file.path(tempdir(), "shennong-pbmc3k-io"))  metadata_path <- file.path(outdir, "pbmc3k_metadata.csv") object_path <- file.path(outdir, "pbmc3k_initialized.qs2")  metadata_export <- cbind(cell = rownames(pbmc[[]]), pbmc[[]]) sn_write(metadata_export, metadata_path, row.names = FALSE) sn_write(pbmc, object_path)  sn_check_file(c(metadata_path, object_path))  metadata <- sn_read(metadata_path, row_names = "cell") pbmc_cached <- sn_read(object_path)  dim(metadata) pbmc_cached pbmc <- sn_convert_bpcells(   pbmc,   directory = file.path(outdir, "pbmc3k_bpcells"),   layers = c("counts", "data"),   overwrite = TRUE )  sn_write(pbmc, file.path(outdir, "pbmc3k_bpcells_bound.qs2"))

title: Data input, output, and project setup
text: intermediate object reference dataset reused another project, upload exact files versioned manifest Zenodo sn_upload_zenodo(). default draft-first: inspect Zenodo draft, rerun publish = TRUE record ready. real upload, set ZENODO_TOKEN ZENODO_SANDBOX_TOKEN remove dry_run = TRUE. uploaded shennong_zenodo_manifest.json records dataset version, Shennong version, file sizes, checksums future analyses can verify reusing data release.
code: zenodo_plan <- sn_upload_zenodo(   files = c(metadata_path, object_path),   title = "PBMC3k initialized Shennong example",   creators = "Duan, Songqi",   version = "2026.05.04",   sandbox = TRUE,   dry_run = TRUE )  zenodo_plan$files[, c("file", "size", "md5")] zenodo_plan$manifest_path

title: Data input, output, and project setup
text: Python workflow exports AnnData metadata embeddings, use sn_add_data_from_anndata() merge files back Seurat object one explicit import step. keeps cross-language handoffs visible analysis script instead leaving undocumented slot edits.
code: pbmc <- sn_add_data_from_anndata(   object = pbmc,   metadata = "results/scanpy/obs.csv",   reductions = c(umap = "results/scanpy/X_umap.csv") )

title: Data input, output, and project setup
text: new analysis repository, sn_initialize_project() creates lightweight project scaffold Shennong/Codex-oriented governance files. Use analysis needs reproducible, just R object. installing optional backends, inspect Shennong considers required, recommended, optional workflow. serialized project objects, prefer .qs2 files new workflows. Legacy .qs files remain supported archived qs package already present active R library, sn_install_dependencies() attempt install qs current R releases. package also ships Codex skill assets Shennong-style analysis projects. helpers expose installed locations without making users search package directory manually.
code: sn_initialize_project(   path = "~/projects/pbmc3k-shennong-demo",   project_name = "pbmc3k-shennong-demo" ) sn_check_version()  sn_install_shennong(source = "github", ref = "main")  deps <- sn_list_dependencies() dplyr::count(deps, scope, source)  sn_install_dependencies(scope = "recommended") sn_get_codex_skill_path() sn_install_codex_skill(destination = ".codex/skills")

title: Interpretation and reporting
text: interpretation functions expect stored results. keeps prompts reproducible: report can say DE result, enrichment result, cluster column used.
code: library(Shennong) library(Seurat) library(dplyr)  pbmc <- sn_load_data("pbmc3k") #> INFO [2026-07-20 06:05:19] Initializing Seurat object for project: pbmc3k. #> INFO [2026-07-20 06:05:19] Running QC metrics for human. #> INFO [2026-07-20 06:05:19] Seurat object initialization complete.  pbmc <- sn_run_cluster(   object = pbmc,   normalization_method = "seurat",   nfeatures = 1500,   dims = 1:15,   resolution = 0.6,   species = "human",   verbose = FALSE )  pbmc <- sn_find_de(   object = pbmc,   analysis = "markers",   group_by = "seurat_clusters",   layer = "data",   store_name = "cluster_markers",   return_object = TRUE,   verbose = FALSE ) sn_list_results(pbmc) #> # A tibble: 1 × 8 #>   collection type  name        analysis method created_at n_rows source #>   <chr>      <chr> <chr>       <chr>    <chr>  <chr>       <int> <chr>  #> 1 de_results de    cluster_ma… markers  wilcox 2026-07-2…   5049 NA

title: Interpretation and reporting
text: Evidence helpers return ordinary R lists tables. Inspect using LLM; step keeps interpretation transparent. enrichment run, add pathway evidence way.
code: annotation_evidence <- sn_prepare_annotation_evidence(   object = pbmc,   de_name = "cluster_markers",   cluster_by = "seurat_clusters",   n_markers = 8,   include_qc = TRUE,   reduction = "umap" )  de_evidence <- sn_prepare_de_evidence(   object = pbmc,   de_name = "cluster_markers",   n_genes = 10 )  results_evidence <- sn_prepare_results_evidence(   object = pbmc,   cluster_de_name = "cluster_markers",   cluster_by = "seurat_clusters",   n_markers = 5 )  names(annotation_evidence) #>  [1] "task"                      "cluster_col"               #>  [3] "source_de_name"            "source_enrichment_name"    #>  [5] "analysis_method"           "species"                   #>  [7] "marker_selection"          "enrichment_selection"      #>  [9] "geometry_reduction"        "cluster_summary"           #> [11] "top_marker_table"          "enrichment_summary"        #> [13] "qc_summary"                "lineage_hints"             #> [15] "canonical_marker_snapshot" "geometry_summary"          #> [17] "caveats" head(annotation_evidence$cluster_summary) #> # A tibble: 6 × 15 #>   cluster n_cells fraction sample_distribution top_markers              #>   <chr>     <int>    <dbl> <chr>               <chr>                    #> 1 0           590   0.214  pbmc3k (590)        AQP3, CD40LG, LMNA, TRA… #> 2 1           563   0.205  pbmc3k (563)        CCR7, APBA2, TRABD2A, T… #> 3 2           479   0.174  pbmc3k (479)        FOLR3, S100A12, S100A8,… #> 4 3           360   0.131  pbmc3k (360)        IGLC3, FCRLA, IGHD, TCL… #> 5 4           345   0.125  pbmc3k (345)        GZMK, TRGC2, CD8A, KLRG… #> 6 5           171   0.0621 pbmc3k (171)        CKB, CDKN1C, RDUR, MS4A… #> # ℹ 10 more variables: median_nFeature_RNA <dbl>, #> #   median_nCount_RNA <dbl>, median_percent.mt <dbl>, #> #   median_percent.ribo <dbl>, median_percent.hb <dbl>, #> #   doublet_fraction <dbl>, heuristic_hint <chr>, #> #   heuristic_rationale <chr>, heuristic_top_signatures <chr>, #> #   nearest_clusters <chr> pbmc <- sn_enrich(   x = pbmc,   source_de_name = "cluster_markers",   species = "human",   database = "GOBP",   store_name = "cluster_gobp",   return_object = TRUE )  enrichment_evidence <- sn_prepare_enrichment_evidence(   object = pbmc,   enrichment_name = "cluster_gobp",   n_terms = 8 )

what: Build a prompt without calling a model
title: Interpretation and reporting
text: sn_build_prompt() useful even plan call model R. gives model-ready human-readable representation exact evidence bundle. high-level helper can return prompt directly.
code: annotation_prompt <- sn_build_prompt(   evidence = annotation_evidence,   task = "annotation",   audience = "scientist",   language = "en",   background = "PBMC3k single-cell RNA-seq clustered with Shennong.",   output_format = "llm",   include_json_schema = TRUE )  names(annotation_prompt) #> [1] "output_format" "task"          "system"        "user"          #> [5] "messages"      "evidence" annotation_prompt$task #> [1] "annotation" annotation_prompt <- sn_interpret_annotation(   object = pbmc,   de_name = "cluster_markers",   cluster_by = "seurat_clusters",   return_prompt = TRUE,   output_format = "llm",   background = "Annotate PBMC3k clusters conservatively." ) #> INFO [2026-07-20 06:06:02] [sn_interpret_annotation] Starting interpretation workflow. #> INFO [2026-07-20 06:06:02] [sn_interpret_annotation] Step 1/5: Preparing annotation evidence (elapsed 0.0s). #> INFO [2026-07-20 06:06:03] [sn_interpret_annotation] Step 2/5: Building annotation prompt (elapsed 0.3s). #> INFO [2026-07-20 06:06:03] [sn_interpret_annotation] Prompt prepared (total elapsed 0.3s).  annotation_prompt$task #> [1] "annotation"

title: Interpretation and reporting
text: Shennong uses provider functions package boundary. can create ellmer provider environment variables, test , pass interpretation helpers.
code: provider <- sn_make_ellmer_provider(   model = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_MODEL", "gpt-5.1"),   reasoning_effort = "medium" )  sn_test_llm_provider(provider = provider)  response <- sn_run_llm(   messages = annotation_prompt$messages,   provider = provider,   model = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_MODEL", "gpt-5.1") )

title: Interpretation and reporting
text: DE enrichment helpers use pattern: return prompt default review, pass provider store response.
code: de_prompt <- sn_interpret_de(   object = pbmc,   de_name = "cluster_markers",   n_genes = 10,   return_prompt = TRUE,   background = "Summarize PBMC3k cluster marker programs." ) #> INFO [2026-07-20 06:06:03] [sn_interpret_de] Starting interpretation workflow. #> INFO [2026-07-20 06:06:03] [sn_interpret_de] Step 1/4: Preparing DE evidence (elapsed 0.0s). #> INFO [2026-07-20 06:06:03] [sn_interpret_de] Step 2/4: Building interpretation prompt (elapsed 0.1s). #> INFO [2026-07-20 06:06:03] [sn_interpret_de] Prompt prepared (total elapsed 0.1s).  de_prompt$task #> [1] "de" enrichment_prompt <- sn_interpret_enrichment(   object = pbmc,   enrichment_name = "cluster_gobp",   n_terms = 8,   return_prompt = TRUE,   background = "Summarize pathway programs by cluster." )

what: Write results, legends, and presentation summaries
title: Interpretation and reporting
text: reporting helpers assemble multiple evidence sources audience-specific outputs. can return prompts manual review store generated responses object@misc$interpretation_results. provider used, retrieve stored outputs name. practical workflow : compute evidence, inspect evidence, build prompt, call provider prompt good enough send.
code: results_prompt <- sn_write_results(   object = pbmc,   cluster_de_name = "cluster_markers",   cluster_by = "seurat_clusters",   return_prompt = TRUE,   background = "Write a concise Results paragraph for PBMC3k clustering." ) #> INFO [2026-07-20 06:06:03] [sn_write_results] Starting interpretation workflow. #> INFO [2026-07-20 06:06:03] [sn_write_results] Step 1/4: Preparing results evidence (elapsed 0.0s). #> INFO [2026-07-20 06:06:03] [sn_write_results] Step 2/4: Building writing prompt (elapsed 0.3s). #> INFO [2026-07-20 06:06:03] [sn_write_results] Prompt prepared (total elapsed 0.3s).  legend_prompt <- sn_write_figure_legend(   object = pbmc,   cluster_de_name = "cluster_markers",   cluster_by = "seurat_clusters",   return_prompt = TRUE,   background = "Describe a UMAP and marker dot plot." ) #> INFO [2026-07-20 06:06:03] [sn_write_figure_legend] Starting interpretation workflow. #> INFO [2026-07-20 06:06:03] [sn_write_figure_legend] Step 1/4: Preparing legend evidence (elapsed 0.0s). #> INFO [2026-07-20 06:06:04] [sn_write_figure_legend] Step 2/4: Building legend prompt (elapsed 0.3s). #> INFO [2026-07-20 06:06:04] [sn_write_figure_legend] Prompt prepared (total elapsed 0.3s).  summary_prompt <- sn_write_presentation_summary(   object = pbmc,   cluster_de_name = "cluster_markers",   cluster_by = "seurat_clusters",   return_prompt = TRUE,   background = "Prepare a short collaborator-facing PBMC3k summary." ) #> INFO [2026-07-20 06:06:04] [sn_write_presentation_summary] Starting interpretation workflow. #> INFO [2026-07-20 06:06:04] [sn_write_presentation_summary] Step 1/4: Preparing presentation evidence (elapsed 0.0s). #> INFO [2026-07-20 06:06:04] [sn_write_presentation_summary] Step 2/4: Building presentation prompt (elapsed 0.3s). #> INFO [2026-07-20 06:06:04] [sn_write_presentation_summary] Prompt prepared (total elapsed 0.3s).  c(results_prompt$task, legend_prompt$task, summary_prompt$task) #> [1] "results"              "figure_legend"        #> [3] "presentation_summary" pbmc <- sn_interpret_de(   object = pbmc,   de_name = "cluster_markers",   provider = provider,   store_name = "cluster_marker_summary",   return_object = TRUE )  sn_get_interpretation_result(   pbmc,   interpretation_name = "cluster_marker_summary" )

title: Layer-aware workflows
text: object still original counts. new layer simply another input matrix layer-aware Shennong functions can target.
code: library(Shennong) library(Seurat) library(dplyr)  pbmc <- sn_load_data("pbmc3k") #> INFO [2026-07-20 06:06:11] Initializing Seurat object for project: pbmc3k. #> INFO [2026-07-20 06:06:11] Running QC metrics for human. #> INFO [2026-07-20 06:06:12] Seurat object initialization complete. counts_full <- SeuratObject::LayerData(pbmc, assay = "RNA", layer = "counts") demo_features <- names(sort(Matrix::rowSums(counts_full), decreasing = TRUE))[   seq_len(min(1000, nrow(counts_full))) ] demo_cells <- colnames(pbmc)[seq_len(min(400, ncol(pbmc)))] pbmc <- subset(pbmc, features = demo_features, cells = demo_cells)  counts <- SeuratObject::LayerData(pbmc, assay = "RNA", layer = "counts") SeuratObject::LayerData(pbmc, assay = "RNA", layer = "decontaminated_counts") <- counts  SeuratObject::Layers(pbmc[["RNA"]]) #> [1] "counts"                "decontaminated_counts"

title: Layer-aware workflows
text: sn_run_cluster() temporarily uses requested layer workflow restores original count layer returning object.
code: pbmc_layered <- sn_run_cluster(   object = pbmc,   normalization_method = "seurat",   assay = "RNA",   layer = "decontaminated_counts",   nfeatures = 500,   dims = 1:10,   resolution = 0.6,   species = "human",   verbose = FALSE ) #> WARN [2026-07-20 06:06:13] Skipping cell cycle scoring because the selected assay has insufficient overlap with human cell-cycle markers (S: 0, G2M: 1).  sn_plot_dim(   pbmc_layered,   reduction = "umap",   group_by = "seurat_clusters",   label = TRUE,   title = "PBMC3k clustered from decontaminated_counts" )

what: Run DE on the right layer
title: Layer-aware workflows
text: Marker contrast analyses usually use normalized data; pseudobulk analyses usually use counts. Shennong keeps distinction explicit layer. stored metadata records analysis context, helps downstream plotting interpretation recover result made.
code: pbmc_layered <- sn_find_de(   object = pbmc_layered,   analysis = "markers",   group_by = "seurat_clusters",   assay = "RNA",   layer = "data",   store_name = "cluster_markers",   return_object = TRUE,   verbose = FALSE )  sn_get_de_result(   pbmc_layered,   de_name = "cluster_markers",   top_n = 3,   with_metadata = TRUE ) #> $schema_version #> [1] "1.0.0" #>  #> $package_version #> [1] "0.2.0.9000" #>  #> $created_at #> [1] "2026-07-20 06:06:19 UTC" #>  #> $table #> #
