what: Use the same layer in other workflows
title: Layer-aware workflows
text: Layer selection appears functions using wrong matrix change answer.
code: pbmc_layered <- sn_filter_genes(   pbmc_layered,   min_cells = 5,   assay = "RNA",   layer = "decontaminated_counts",   species = "human" )  pbmc_layered <- sn_run_celltypist(   x = pbmc_layered,   assay = "RNA",   layer = "decontaminated_counts",   over_clustering = "seurat_clusters",   quiet = TRUE )  rogue_tbl <- sn_calculate_rogue(   pbmc_layered,   cluster_by = "seurat_clusters",   assay = "RNA",   layer = "decontaminated_counts" )

title: Layer-aware workflows
text: Simulation useful need controlled object covariate structure real experiment. sn_simulate() keeps API method-based, new simulation engines can added later. method = "scdesign3", converts selected Seurat layer SingleCellExperiment, calls scDesign3, returns simulated Seurat object default. merge original simulated cells unless combine_original = TRUE.
code: sim_pbmc <- sn_simulate(   object = pbmc_layered,   method = "scdesign3",   celltype = "seurat_clusters",   ncell = 100,   assay = "RNA",   layer = "decontaminated_counts",   n_cores = 1,   return = "seurat" )  sim_pbmc sim_pbmc@misc$scdesign3

title: Layer-aware workflows
text: Layer-aware results still regular Seurat objects tables, IO helpers first article apply. main habit name layer every step matters. makes workflow readable months later reduces chance silently switching raw corrected matrices.
code: outdir <- sn_set_path(file.path(tempdir(), "shennong-pbmc3k-layered"))  object_path <- file.path(outdir, "pbmc3k_layered.qs2") markers_path <- file.path(outdir, "pbmc3k_layered_markers.csv")  sn_write(pbmc_layered, object_path) sn_write(   sn_get_de_result(pbmc_layered, "cluster_markers", top_n = 5),   markers_path )  sn_check_file(c(object_path, markers_path))

title: Integration metrics and diagnostics
code: library(Shennong) library(Seurat) library(dplyr)  pbmc <- sn_load_data("pbmc3k") #> INFO [2026-07-20 06:06:27] Initializing Seurat object for project: pbmc3k. #> INFO [2026-07-20 06:06:27] Running QC metrics for human. #> INFO [2026-07-20 06:06:27] Seurat object initialization complete. pbmc$library <- rep(c("library_a", "library_b"), length.out = ncol(pbmc))  pbmc <- sn_run_cluster(   object = pbmc,   batch = "library",   normalization_method = "seurat",   hvg_group_by = "library",   nfeatures = 1500,   dims = 1:15,   resolution = 0.6,   species = "human",   verbose = FALSE )

title: Integration metrics and diagnostics
text: LISI asks diverse cell’s local neighborhood respect label. function can measure library mixing cluster mixing changing label. Silhouette asks whether label compact embedding. biological labels, high silhouette can good; batch labels, high silhouette often means residual batch separation. Graph connectivity asks whether cells label remain connected neighbor graph.
code: library_lisi <- sn_calculate_lisi(   pbmc,   reduction = "harmony",   label_by = "library",   dims = 1:15,   max_cells = 1500 )  cluster_lisi <- sn_calculate_lisi(   pbmc,   reduction = "harmony",   label_by = "seurat_clusters",   dims = 1:15,   max_cells = 1500 )  head(library_lisi) #>            cell_id  library #> 1 AAACATACAACCAC-1 1.851349 #> 2 AAACATTGATCAGC-1 1.763703 #> 3 AAACCGTGCTTCCG-1 1.693069 #> 4 AAACGCACTGGTAC-1 1.599863 #> 5 AAACGCTGACCAGT-1 1.818432 #> 6 AAACGCTGGTTCTT-1 1.998104 head(cluster_lisi) #>            cell_id seurat_clusters #> 1 AAACATTGAGCTAC-1 1.000000 cluster_sil <- sn_calculate_silhouette(   pbmc,   label_by = "seurat_clusters",   reduction = "harmony",   dims = 1:15,   max_cells = 1500 )  head(cluster_sil) #>            cell_id seurat_clusters silhouette_width #> 1 AAACATTGAGCTAC-1               4       0.42128412 #> 2 AAACATTGATCAGC-1               1       0.23931000 #> 3 AAACGCTGACCAGT-1               3       0.10464782 #> sn_calculate_graph_connectivity(   pbmc,   label_by = "seurat_clusters",   reduction = "harmony",   dims = 1:15,   max_cells = 1500 )  connectivity #>    seurat_clusters n_cells largest_component what: Compare clusters to labels when references exist
title: Integration metrics and diagnostics
text: trusted label column, purity, entropy, clustering agreement show computed clusters relate reference. artificial label deliberately simple. real workflow, replace cell-type annotations, reference-mapping labels, curated manual labels.
code: pbmc$coarse_label <- paste0("cluster_", pbmc$seurat_clusters)  sn_calculate_cluster_purity(   pbmc,   cluster_by = "seurat_clusters",   label_by = "coarse_label" ) #>    seurat_clusters n_cells dominant_label dominant_label_n #> 1                3     368      cluster_3              368 #> 2                4     357      cluster_4              357 #> 3                1     527      cluster_1              527 #> 4                2     487      cluster_2              487 #> 5                6     154      cluster_6              154 #> 6                5     165      cluster_5 title: Integration metrics and diagnostics
text: PCR batch scoring measures much variation embedding explained batch. Supplying baseline reduction lets compare correction pre-integration PCA space. several metadata variables might drive residual structure, rank directly variance-explained scoring. Use single-variable mode quick screen, partial mode design fully confounded.
code: pcr <- sn_calculate_pcr_batch(   pbmc,   batch = "library",   reduction = "harmony",   baseline_reduction = "pca",   dims = 1:15,   max_cells = 1500 )  pcr #>   reduction baseline_reduction batch_column n_cells batch_variance #> 1   harmony                pca      library    1500   0.0002694834 #>   baseline_batch_variance pcr_improvement scaled_score #> 1            0.0002895414    2.005803e-05   0.06927517 pbmc$study <- paste0("study_", pbmc$library) pbmc$tissue <- "blood" pbmc$sample <- rep(paste0("donor_", seq_len(4)), length.out = ncol(pbmc))  variance_drivers <- sn_calculate_variance_explained(   pbmc,   variables = c("library", "study", "tissue", "sample"),   reduction = "pca",   dims = 1:15,   max_cells = 1500 )  variance_drivers #>   variable reduction method n_cells n_dims n_levels variance_explained #> 1   sample       pca single    1500     15        4       0.0011152765 #> 2  library       pca single    1500     15        2       0.0002026187 #> 3    study       pca single    1500     15        2       0.0002026187 #> 4   tissue       pca single    1500     15        1       0.0000000000 #>   mean_dim_variance_explained max_dim_variance_explained #> 1                 0.002004462                0.006997445 #> 2                 0.000441611                0.002234708 #> 3                 0.000441611                0.002234708 #> 4                 0.000000000                0.000000000

title: Integration metrics and diagnostics
text: sn_identify_challenging_groups() combines rarity, connectivity, local mixing diagnostics surface groups deserve manual review. useful final annotation. ROGUE optional cluster-purity diagnostic depends upstream ROGUE package. Keep targeted check rather mandatory build step. intended workflow : use aggregate table quick read, drill single metrics value changes downstream decision.

what: Load PBMC3k and inspect species-aware QC fields
title: Preprocessing and QC with PBMC3k
text: sn_load_data("pbmc3k") downloads reuses cached 10x H5 file, reads , initializes Seurat object human QC metrics. start raw matrix instead, equivalent explicit form : gene identifiers mixed symbol styles concern, standardize initialization call sn_standardize_gene_symbols() downstream analysis. HGNChelper provide unambiguous replacement otherwise valid symbol, Shennong preserves original symbol instead returning NA feature name.
code: library(Shennong) library(Seurat) library(dplyr)  pbmc <- sn_load_data("pbmc3k") #> INFO [2026-07-20 06:06:49] Initializing Seurat object for project: pbmc3k. #> INFO [2026-07-20 06:06:49] Running QC metrics for human. #> INFO [2026-07-20 06:06:49] Seurat object initialization complete. pbmc$sample <- "pbmc3k"  sn_get_species(pbmc) #> [1] "human"  head(pbmc[[]][, c("sample", "nCount_RNA", "nFeature_RNA", "percent.mt")]) #>                  sample nCount_RNA nFeature_RNA percent.mt #> AAACATACAACCAC-1 pbmc3k       2844         1046  2.7777778 #> AAACATTGAGCTAC-1 pbmc3k       5717         1722  3.2534546 #> AAACATTGATCAGC-1 pbmc3k       3766         1500  0.6903877 #> AAACCGTGCTTCCG-1 pbmc3k       2981         1153  1.5095606 #> AAACCGTGTATGCG-1 pbmc3k       1229          698  0.8950366 #> AAACGCACTGGTAC-1 pbmc3k       2536         1032  1.4984227 counts <- sn_read("pbmc3k_filtered_feature_bc_matrix.h5")  pbmc <- sn_initialize_seurat_object(   x = counts,   project = "pbmc3k_demo",   sample_name = "pbmc3k",   study = "10x_pbmc",   species = "human" ) pbmc <- sn_standardize_gene_symbols(   pbmc,   species = "human",   is_gene_id = FALSE )

what: Filter cells with inspectable QC flags
title: Preprocessing and QC with PBMC3k
text: sn_filter_cells() uses median absolute deviation thresholds default. key point filtering flagging separated: set filter = FALSE see cells removed, set filter = TRUE rule acceptable. flags look sensible, apply rule:
code: pbmc_flagged <- sn_filter_cells(   x = pbmc,   features = c("nFeature_RNA", "nCount_RNA", "percent.mt"),   method = "mad",   n = c(5, 5, 3),   plot = FALSE,   filter = FALSE )  qc_flag_cols <- grep("^qc_", colnames(pbmc_flagged[[]]), value = TRUE) head(pbmc_flagged[[]][, qc_flag_cols, drop = FALSE]) #> data frame with 0 columns and 6 rows pbmc_cells <- sn_filter_cells(   x = pbmc,   features = c("nFeature_RNA", "nCount_RNA", "percent.mt"),   method = "mad",   n = c(5, 5, 3),   plot = FALSE,   filter = TRUE )  c(before = ncol(pbmc), after = ncol(pbmc_cells)) #> before  after  #>   2753   2597

what: Filter genes by expression and annotation
title: Preprocessing and QC with PBMC3k
text: sn_filter_genes() keeps threshold optional annotation rule one place. expression threshold protects sparse downstream steps, gene_class = "coding" can remove noncoding genes workflow protein-coding focused.
code: pbmc_genes <- sn_filter_genes(   x = pbmc_cells,   min_cells = 5,   plot = FALSE,   filter = TRUE,   species = "human",   gene_class = "coding" ) #> WARN [2026-07-20 06:06:52] Annotation-based gene filtering could not match 17 features for species 'human'. Those unmatched features will be dropped. Examples: LINC01115.1, PCBP1-AS1.1, LSP1P5.1, DDX11L2.1, LINC01618.1, CAST.1, RAET1E-AS1.1, LINC03021.1, LINC03023.1, BMS1P14.1.  c(before = nrow(pbmc_cells), after = nrow(pbmc_genes)) #> before  after  #>  54872  12403

title: Preprocessing and QC with PBMC3k
text: sn_normalize_data() sn_score_cell_cycle() keep preprocessing inside Shennong vocabulary used later clustering plotting.
code: pbmc_norm <- sn_normalize_data(   object = pbmc_genes,   normalization_method = "seurat",   verbose = FALSE )  pbmc_norm <- sn_score_cell_cycle(pbmc_norm, species = "human")  table(pbmc_norm$Phase) #>  #>  G1 G2M   S  #> 872 896 829

title: Preprocessing and QC with PBMC3k
text: Doublet detection ambient correction depend optional backends. Shennong keeps explicit steps rather hiding clustering. ambient RNA, PBMC3k raw counts can loaded example registry. corrected matrix written new layer default, original counts remain inspectable.
code: pbmc_doublets <- sn_find_doublets(pbmc_norm) table(pbmc_doublets$doublet_class) pbmc_raw <- sn_load_data("pbmc3k", matrix_type = "raw")  pbmc_decont <- sn_remove_ambient_contamination(   x = pbmc_norm,   raw = pbmc_raw,   method = "decontx",   layer = "decontaminated_counts",   verbose = FALSE )

title: Preprocessing and QC with PBMC3k
text: sn_assess_qc() summarizes object filtering can compare pre-filter object. Store result want later reports recover QC decision without rerunning preprocessing. object now ready clustering. important part just cells genes filtered, rationale summary stored place downstream code can find.
code: qc_report <- sn_assess_qc(   object = pbmc_norm,   reference = pbmc,   sample_by = "sample",   verbose = FALSE )  qc_report$overall #>   n_samples n_cells qc_score qc_label retention_fraction #> 1         1    2597 84.92677     good          0.9433345 #>   low_quality_removed_fraction doublet_removed_fraction #> 1                          NaN                      NaN  pbmc_norm <- sn_assess_qc(   object = pbmc_norm,   reference = pbmc,   sample_by = "sample",   store_name = "pbmc3k_preprocessing",   return_object = TRUE,   verbose = FALSE )  names(pbmc_norm@misc$qc_assessments) #> [1] "pbmc3k_preprocessing"

title: Program Discovery and Gene Regulatory Networks
text: local NMF backend uses several random restarts, retains best reconstruction, stores restart cross-run stability diagnostics. Use group_by stratum can support independent factorization. cNMF Hotspot require explicit runner imported result; external environment never installed silently.
code: object <- sn_discover_programs(   object,   method = "nmf",   n_programs = 6,   name = "tumor_programs",   backend_control = list(nrun = 10, seed = 717) )  programs <- sn_get_result(object, "program_discovery", "tumor_programs") head(programs$tables$gene_weights) programs$tables$fit_diagnostics programs$tables$stability  sn_plot_discovered_programs(object, "tumor_programs", type = "weights") sn_plot_discovered_programs(object, "tumor_programs", type = "activity")

title: Program Discovery and Gene Regulatory Networks
text: GENIE3 directly runnable R backend. Supplying curated regulator list usually produces interpretable network reduces compute cost. pySCENIC, run motif-pruning workflow audited Python environment pass edge/AUCell tables backend_control$runner backend_control$result. legacy R SCENIC adapter exists prior result exports; presented default runtime. default derived activity mean expression regulon’s retained targets. backend_control = list(activity_method = "ucell") selects rank-based activity. group_by supplied, Shennong reports eta-squared effect size alongside group means; deliberately label statistic SCENIC’s regulon-specificity score.
code: object <- sn_run_grn(   object,   method = "genie3",   regulators = transcription_factors,   group_by = "cell_type",   name = "tumor_grn" )  grn <- sn_get_result(object, "grn", "tumor_grn") head(grn$tables$edges) head(grn$tables$regulons) head(grn$tables$activity) head(grn$tables$specificity)  sn_plot_regulon(object, "tumor_grn", type = "network") sn_plot_regulon(object, "tumor_grn", type = "specificity")

title: Publication figure specifications and export bundles
text: Generic profiles describe screen, column, page, slide constraints. claim replace current journal artwork instructions; verify submission. five-million-point example calculates metadata ; allocate render five million rows. Point size follows clamped power rule, high-density layers recommended rasterization text geometry remain vector PDF/SVG output.
code: sn_list_figure_profiles() #> # A tibble: 6 × 7 #>   profile     width_mm height_mm max_height_mm base_font_pt min_font_pt #>   <chr>          <dbl>     <dbl>         <dbl>        <dbl>       <dbl> #> 1 screen           160       100           180           10           8 #> 2 single_col…       85        NA           120            7           6 #> 3 one_half_c…      120        NA           160            7           6 #> 4 double_col…      180        NA           220            7           6 #> 5 supplement…      180        NA           240            7           6 #> 6 slide_16_9       254       143           143           16          12 #> # ℹ 1 more variable: raster_dpi <dbl>  large_spec <- sn_figure_spec(   plot_type = "embedding",   data_summary = list(     n_points = 5000000,     n_groups = 18,     n_panels = 1,     max_label_chars = 16   ),   profile = "single_column" ) large_spec$recommended #> $width_mm #> [1] 85 #>  #> $height_mm #> [1] 87 #>  #> $point_size #> [1] 0.061 #>  #> $alpha #> [1] 0.55 #>  #> $font_size_pt #> [1] 7 #>  #> $line_width #> [1] 0.35 #>  #> $legend_position #> [1] "bottom" #>  #> $rasterize #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $raster_dpi #> [1] 600 #>  #> $panel_layout #> $panel_layout$columns #> [1] 1 #>  #> $panel_layout$rows #> [1] 1 #>  #>  #> $pagination #> $pagination$required #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $pagination$pages #> [1] 1 #>  #> $pagination$per_page #> [1] NA #>  #> $pagination$axis #> [1] NA

title: Publication figure specifications and export bundles
text: Core Shennong plots attach shennong_figure_spec automatically. native plot can also profiled construction. Validation reports calculated dimensions, text raster settings, small panels, category/legend overload, long labels, pagination, extreme aspect ratios, dense networks. predicts likely problems; final visual review still required.
code: plot <- ggplot2::ggplot(   mtcars,   ggplot2::aes(wt, mpg, color = factor(cyl)) ) +   ggplot2::geom_point()  plot <- sn_apply_figure_profile(plot, "single_column") sn_figure_spec(plot)$recommended #> $width_mm #> [1] 85 #>  #> $height_mm #> [1] 85 #>  #> $point_size #> [1] 1.6 #>  #> $alpha #> [1] 0.85 #>  #> $font_size_pt #> [1] 7 #>  #> $line_width #> [1] 0.35 #>  #> $legend_position #> [1] "right" #>  #> $rasterize #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $raster_dpi #> [1] 600 #>  #> $panel_layout #> $panel_layout$columns #> [1] 1 #>  #> $panel_layout$rows #> [1] 1 #>  #>  #> $pagination #> $pagination$required #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $pagination$pages #> [1] 1 #>  #> $pagination$per_page #> [1] NA #>  #> $pagination$axis #> [1] NA sn_validate_figure(plot) #> Figure validation: PASS #> - width: 85 mm #> - height: 85 mm #> - font_size: 7 pt #> - raster_dpi: 600 dpi #> - panel_width: 85 mm #> - panel_height: 85 mm

title: Publication figure specifications and export bundles
text: sn_save_figure() supports PDF, SVG, TIFF, PNG without depending current RStudio graphics device. Explicit dimensions override automatic values. reproducible handoff, export rendering, source table, calculated specification, session information, checksums, validation result together. Result-aware sn_plot_de(), sn_plot_enrichment(), sn_plot_gsea() attach standardized plotting tables bundle source data. QC, clustering, integration, annotation projection, bulk, dimensional, feature, dot, heatmap, violin, box, bar, composition, Milo helpers also expose figure metadata automatic sizing QA.
code: sn_save_figure(   plot,   "figures/Figure_1A.pdf",   profile = "single_column" ) manifest <- sn_export_figure_bundle(   plot,   path = "figures/Figure_1A",   formats = c("pdf", "png"),   include_data = TRUE,   include_spec = TRUE,   include_session = TRUE,   profile = "single_column" ) manifest$files

title: Spatial Feature, Domain, and Neighborhood Workflows
text: Use method = "nnsvg" optional nearest-neighbor Gaussian-process backend. SPARK-X enters explicit runner/result adapter.
code: object <- sn_find_spatial_features(   object,   method = "morans_i",   spatial_cols = c("spatial_x", "spatial_y"),   backend_control = list(k = 6, n_permutations = 999, seed = 717) )  svg <- sn_get_result(object, "spatial_features", "spatial_features") head(svg$tables$features) head(svg$graphs$spatial) sn_plot_spatial_svg(svg) sn_plot_spatial_feature(object, svg$tables$features$feature[1:4])

title: Spatial Feature, Domain, and Neighborhood Workflows
text: local graph avoids allocating full --distance matrix. Squidpy available heavier backend graph ecosystem needed.
code: object <- sn_find_spatial_domains(   object,   method = "banksy",   store_name = "banksy_domains",   backend_control = list(lambda = 0.8, resolution = 1, seed = 717) ) sn_plot_spatial_domain(object, "banksy_domains")  object <- sn_run_spatial_neighborhood(   object,   group_by = "cell_type",   backend_control = list(k = 6, n_permutations = 999) ) neighborhood <- sn_get_result(object, "spatial_neighborhood", "spatial_neighborhood") sn_plot_spatial_neighborhood(neighborhood, type = "enrichment") sn_plot_spatial_neighborhood(neighborhood, type = "cooccurrence")

title: Spatial Feature, Domain, and Neighborhood Workflows
text: Run sn_run_cell_communication() first. augment evidence spatial distances: Distance filtering create ligand-receptor evidence; adds proximity constraint already auditable communication result.
code: object <- sn_run_spatial_communication(   object,   communication_name = "communication",   group_by = "cell_type",   max_distance = 200,   store_name = "spatial_communication" )  spatial_communication <- sn_get_result(   object, "spatial_communication", "spatial_communication" ) head(spatial_communication$tables$group_distances) sn_plot_spatial_communication(spatial_communication)

title: Trajectory, Pseudotime, and Dynamic Genes
text: start end cluster labels. expected topology, provide named cluster paths lineages; Shennong constrains endpoints records warning Slingshot recover path exactly.
code: library(Shennong)  object <- sn_run_trajectory(   object,   reduction = "pca",   cluster_by = "seurat_clusters",   start = "naive",   end = c("effector", "memory"),   store_name = "t_cell_development" )

what: Discover and retrieve the stored result
title: Trajectory, Pseudotime, and Dynamic Genes
text: cell table contains primary pseudotime plus pseudotime probability every lineage. object metadata also contains t_cell_development_pseudotime t_cell_development_lineage direct use Seurat.
code: sn_list_results(object, type = "trajectory")  trajectory <- sn_get_result(   object,   type = "trajectory",   name = "t_cell_development" )  trajectory$tables$cells trajectory$tables$terminal_states trajectory$graphs$lineages trajectory$diagnostics

title: Trajectory, Pseudotime, and Dynamic Genes
text: default, tradeSeq tests variable features available, capped 2,000. Use dynamic_features define explicit auditable set. Raw counts remain sparse Shennong boundary; tradeSeq controls backend conversion.
code: trajectory$tables$dynamic_genes |>   dplyr::arrange(adjusted_p_value)  trajectory$tables$branch_genes |>   dplyr::filter(test == "pattern") |>   dplyr::arrange(adjusted_p_value)  trajectory$tables$fitted_trends trajectory$tables$convergence

title: Trajectory, Pseudotime, and Dynamic Genes
text: test_dynamic = FALSE runs trajectory inference without tradeSeq. useful topology review committing compute gene-wise models.
code: sn_plot_trajectory(object, "t_cell_development") sn_plot_pseudotime(object, "t_cell_development", lineage = "Lineage1") sn_plot_lineage_probability(object, "t_cell_development", lineage = "Lineage1") sn_plot_dynamic_heatmap(object, "t_cell_development") sn_plot_gene_trend(object, "t_cell_development", c("GZMB", "TCF7")) sn_plot_branch_comparison(object, "t_cell_development", test = "pattern")

title: Trajectory, Pseudotime, and Dynamic Genes
text: Monocle 3 can run directly optional R package installed. root cluster still required pseudotime orientation explicit: Palantir results can produced project-controlled Python environment standardized without hiding boundary. Supply either backend_control$runner backend_control$result; result must contain named per-cell pseudotime, can additionally contain lineage weights, lineages, terminal_states, curves, backend model summary.
code: monocle <- sn_run_trajectory(   object,   method = "monocle3",   reduction = "umap",   cluster_by = "seurat_clusters",   start = "naive",   test_dynamic = FALSE,   return_object = FALSE ) monocle$tables$cells palantir <- sn_run_trajectory(   object,   method = "palantir",   reduction = "pca",   cluster_by = "seurat_clusters",   test_dynamic = FALSE,   backend_control = list(result = palantir_result),   return_object = FALSE ) sn_validate_result(palantir)

title: RNA Velocity and Fate Mapping
text: Seurat object must contain raw spliced unspliced layers shared features cells, plus dimensional reduction used project high-dimensional velocity vectors.
code: sn_prepare_pixi_environment(   "trajectory",   install_environment = TRUE )

title: RNA Velocity and Fate Mapping
text: Low confidence, weak splicing signal, unstable directionality must remain visible interpretation. H5AD artifact recorded models$artifacts contains transition evidence consumed CellRank.
code: object <- sn_run_velocity(   object,   spliced_layer = "spliced",   unspliced_layer = "unspliced",   reduction = "umap",   store_name = "velocity",   backend_control = list(     velocity_mode = "stochastic",     n_neighbors = 30,     seed = 717   ) )  velocity <- sn_get_result(object, "velocity", "velocity") head(velocity$tables$cells) head(velocity$tables$transition_edges) velocity$diagnostics sn_plot_velocity(object, "velocity", color_by = "confidence")

what: Add a regulatory prior with RegVelo
title: RNA Velocity and Fate Mapping
text: RegVelo uses spliced/unspliced embedding inputs, plus explicit gene-regulatory prior. Supply either regulator-target edge table, named target--regulator matrix, CSV path. Shennong retains trained model, H5AD, prior-edge count, velocity vectors, transition graph, RegVelo latent time standard velocity result contract. prior GRN modeling assumption must versioned analysis. interpret smooth vector field independent validation prior.
code: prior_grn <- data.frame(   regulator = c("SOX10", "SOX10", "TFAP2A"),   target = c("ERBB3", "PLP1", "SOX10"),   weight = c(1, 0.8, 1) )  object <- sn_run_velocity(   object,   method = "regvelo",   spliced_layer = "spliced",   unspliced_layer = "unspliced",   reduction = "umap",   store_name = "regvelo",   backend_control = list(     prior_grn = prior_grn,     max_epochs = 1500,     early_stopping = TRUE,     seed = 717   ) )  regvelo <- sn_get_result(object, "velocity", "regvelo") regvelo$tables$cells regvelo$models$artifacts

title: RNA Velocity and Fate Mapping
text: CellRank can consume retained H5AD produced either scVelo RegVelo. macrostate meets default stability threshold, CellRank raises actionable error. documented workflow may use terminal_method = "top_n", terminal_n_states, explicit terminal_states; Shennong records choices result parameters rather silently relaxing rule.
code: object <- sn_run_fate(   object,   velocity_name = "velocity",   store_name = "fate",   backend_control = list(     n_states = 6,     terminal_method = "stability"   ) )  fate <- sn_get_result(object, "fate", "fate") head(fate$tables$terminal_states) head(fate$tables$probabilities) head(fate$tables$lineage_drivers) sn_plot_fate(object, "fate")

title: Visualization with Shennong
text: plot wrappers expect Seurat object produced analysis workflow. fast demonstration, example clusters PBMC3k fewer PCs variable features full analysis.
code: library(Shennong) library(Seurat) library(dplyr) library(ggplot2)  pbmc <- sn_load_data("pbmc3k") #> INFO [2026-07-20 06:07:10] Initializing Seurat object for project: pbmc3k. #> INFO [2026-07-20 06:07:11] Running QC metrics for human. #> INFO [2026-07-20 06:07:11] Seurat object initialization complete.  pbmc <- sn_run_cluster(   object = pbmc,   normalization_method = "seurat",   nfeatures = 1500,   npcs = 20,   dims = 1:15,   resolution = 0.6,   species = "human",   verbose = FALSE )

what: Dimensional maps: labels, palettes, and rasterization
title: Visualization with Shennong
text: sn_plot_dim() default map categorical metadata. hides axes default, chooses practical point size number cells, uses named palette registry instead requiring every script hand-code colors.  need colors programmatically, use registry directly.
code: sn_plot_dim(   object = pbmc,   reduction = "umap",   group_by = "seurat_clusters",   label = TRUE,   label_halo = FALSE,   palette = "Paired",   title = "PBMC3k clusters" ) sn_list_palettes() cluster_cols <- sn_get_palette(   palette = "Paired",   n = length(levels(pbmc$seurat_clusters)),   palette_type = "discrete" )  cluster_cols #>  [1] "#A6CEE3" "#1F78B4" "#B2DF8A" "#33A02C" "#FB9A99" "#E31A1C" #>  [7] "#FDBF6F" "#FF7F00" "#CAB2D6" "#6A3D9A"

what: Feature maps: expression and density modes
title: Visualization with Shennong
text: sn_plot_feature() covers standard expression maps density-style maps. function handles legends, shared scales, rasterization, panel sizing. Rasterization enabled default; ggrastr available point layer rasterized regular ggplot point size applied, small values pt_size = 0.01 behave like ordinary geom_point() sizes. ggrastr unavailable, plot remains usable vector point layer.  Density mode useful expression sparse want emphasize marker-defined state sits embedding.
code: sn_plot_feature(   object = pbmc,   features = c("IL7R", "MS4A1", "LYZ"),   reduction = "umap",   mode = "expression",   palette = "YlOrRd",   title = "Canonical PBMC markers" ) sn_plot_feature(   object = pbmc,   features = "IL7R",   reduction = "umap",   mode = "density",   density_style = "galaxy",   title = "IL7R density" )

title: Visualization with Shennong
text: question known marker panel, heatmap usually clearer another embedding. sn_plot_heatmap() validates requested genes can draw either cell-level group-averaged heatmaps. default mode = "cells" follows Seurat’s heatmap backend hides cell names ticks, uses 8 pt group label, rasterizes default, colors group bar Paired palette.  Use mode = "average" comparison group-level pattern rather every individual cell. averages expression within group z-scores gene default, giving compact marker-panel summary.  Use split_by marker panel inspected separately across samples, conditions, batches.
code: sn_plot_heatmap(   object = pbmc,   features = c("IL7R", "CCR7", "MS4A1", "CD79A", "LYZ", "NKG7"),   group_by = "seurat_clusters",   palette = "RdBu",   label_angle = 45 ) sn_plot_heatmap(   object = pbmc,   features = c("IL7R", "CCR7", "MS4A1", "CD79A", "LYZ", "NKG7"),   group_by = "seurat_clusters",   mode = "average",   palette = "RdBu" ) sn_plot_heatmap(   object = pbmc,   features = c("IL7R", "CCR7", "MS4A1", "CD79A", "LYZ", "NKG7"),   group_by = "seurat_clusters",   split_by = "sample" )

what: Distribution plots for metadata and scores
title: Visualization with Shennong
text: Shennong wraps common summary plots report can move cell-level maps sample-level summaries without switching visual grammar.
code: pbmc$sample <- rep(paste0("donor", 1:6), length.out = ncol(pbmc)) pbmc$condition <- ifelse(pbmc$sample %in% paste0("donor", 1:3), "control", "stim")  sn_plot_violin(   object = pbmc,   features = c("nFeature_RNA", "percent.mt"),   group_by = "condition",   palette = "Set2" ) qc_summary <- pbmc[[]] |>   dplyr::group_by(sample, condition) |>   dplyr::summarise(     median_features = median(nFeature_RNA),     median_mito = median(percent.mt),     .groups = "drop"   )  sn_plot_boxplot(   qc_summary,   x = condition,   y = median_features,   y_label = "Median detected genes",   panel_widths = 80,   panel_heights = 70 )

what: Marker dot plots from stored DE results
title: Visualization with Shennong
text: DE workflow can store markers object@misc$de_results. stored, sn_plot_dot(features = "top_markers") can reuse result directly instead asking users manually copy marker lists scripts.  curated marker panel, pass named list. list element becomes free-width facet, marker families remain visually separated even groups contain different numbers genes.
code: pbmc <- sn_find_de(   object = pbmc,   analysis = "markers",   group_by = "seurat_clusters",   layer = "data",   store_name = "cluster_markers",   return_object = TRUE,   verbose = FALSE )  sn_plot_dot(   x = pbmc,   features = "top_markers",   de_name = "cluster_markers",   group_by = "seurat_clusters",   n = 4,   palette = "Purples" ) level1_markers <- list(   "Lymphoid" = c("CD3D", "CD3E", "MS4A1"),   "Myeloid" = c("LYZ", "FCGR3A"),   "Platelets" = c("PPBP") )  sn_plot_dot(   x = pbmc,   features = level1_markers,   group_by = "seurat_clusters" )

title: Visualization with Shennong
text: sn_calculate_composition() creates table; sn_plot_composition() displays . Keeping steps separate makes denominator grouping explicit.
code: composition <- sn_calculate_composition(   x = pbmc,   group_by = "sample",   variable = "seurat_clusters",   measure = "both",   min_cells = 10 )  sn_plot_composition(   composition,   x = sample,   fill = seurat_clusters,   y = proportion,   position = "stack",   palette = "Paired" )

title: Visualization with Shennong
text: plot covered sn_plot_*() wrapper, use plain ggplot2 resolve colors Shennong. catplot installed, compact theme good final layer manuscript-style figures.
code: condition_cols <- sn_get_palette("Set2", n = 2, palette_type = "discrete") names(condition_cols) <- c("control", "stim")  p <- ggplot(qc_summary, aes(x = condition, y = median_mito, fill = condition)) +   geom_col(width = 0.7) +   scale_fill_manual(values = condition_cols) +   labs(x = NULL, y = "Median mitochondrial percent")  if (requireNamespace("catplot", quietly = TRUE)) {   p <- p + catplot::theme_cat(panel_widths = 80, panel_heights = 70) }  p

title: Toolkit for Interpreting Omics Data
text: Shennong experimental R package single-cell, multimodal, spatial, bulk transcriptomics workflows. Seurat objects remain primary single-cell contract, standalone bulk analyses accept ordinary matrices SummarizedExperiment objects. package provides reproducible entry points preprocessing, integration, annotation, differential testing, biological-state modeling, publication figures, interpretation-ready result storage.

title: Toolkit for Interpreting Omics Data
text: Install current development version GitHub: List Shennong’s required recommended R packages, install missing ones one step:
code: install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("zerostwo/shennong") deps <- sn_list_dependencies() deps  sn_install_dependencies(scope = "required")

title: Toolkit for Interpreting Omics Data
text: Shennong provides stable sn_* entry points R packages, command-line programs, Python workflows. table summarizes current analysis surface data-analysis module. “Managed pixi/CLI” means Shennong prepares input, runs backend project-independent environment, imports result. “Adapter” means Shennong standardizes existing result result returned user-supplied runner; external software silently installed executed. methods shipped inst/methods/ currently implemented Shennong entry point explicit adapter. Optional R packages, command-line programs, pixi environments, credentials, references, model files still required selected backend depends . Inspect registry current machine starting workflow:
code: # Every registered backend and whether it can run in the current session sn_list_methods() sn_list_methods(task = "trajectory") sn_list_methods(available = TRUE)  # Runtime, dependency, install action, inputs and outputs for one backend sn_method_status("cellrank", task = "fate")  # Install missing R dependencies or prepare a managed Python environment sn_install_dependencies(scope = "recommended") sn_prepare_pixi_environment("trajectory", install_environment = TRUE)

title: Toolkit for Interpreting Omics Data
text: Shennong ships installable Agent Skills plus read-MCP server. MCP surface lets agent discover registered methods, inspect exact installed R help, retrieve workflow recipes; execute arbitrary R code modify analysis files.
code: # Install all packaged Shennong usage skills for local agents. sn_install_codex_skill(path = "~/.agents/skills", type = "package_skills")  # Use this command/argument pair in any stdio-capable MCP client. sn_mcp_server_config()  # Equivalent direct server command: # Rscript -e 'Shennong::sn_mcp_server()'

title: Toolkit for Interpreting Omics Data
text: package ships small built-PBMC example derived pbmc1k pbmc3k assets: pbmc_small: Seurat object sample metadata pbmc_small_raw: matching raw count matrix extra droplets larger example datasets, sn_load_data() can still download full PBMC references demand.

title: Toolkit for Interpreting Omics Data
code: library(Shennong)  data("pbmc_small", package = "Shennong")  pbmc <- sn_filter_cells(   pbmc,   features = c("nFeature_RNA", "nCount_RNA", "percent.mt"),   plot = FALSE ) pbmc <- sn_filter_genes(pbmc, min_cells = 3, plot = FALSE) pbmc <- sn_run_cluster(   pbmc,   normalization_method = "seurat",   resolution = 0.6 )  sn_plot_dim(pbmc, group_by = "seurat_clusters", label = TRUE)

title: Toolkit for Interpreting Omics Data
text: built-PBMC example can used batch-aware integration: can summarize integration quality surface rare difficult groups directly integrated object:
code: pbmc_integrated <- sn_run_cluster(   pbmc,   batch = "sample",   normalization_method = "seurat",   resolution = 0.6 )  sn_plot_dim(pbmc_integrated, group_by = "sample") sn_plot_dim(pbmc_integrated, group_by = "seurat_clusters", label = TRUE) metrics <- sn_assess_integration(   pbmc_integrated,   batch = "sample",   cluster = "seurat_clusters",   reduction = "harmony",   baseline_reduction = "pca" )  metrics$summary metrics$per_group$isolated_label_score metrics$per_group$cluster_entropy metrics$per_group$cluster_purity metrics$per_group$challenging_groups

title: Toolkit for Interpreting Omics Data
text: sn_find_de() entry point accepts feature--sample matrix, list, SummarizedExperiment standalone bulk analysis:
code: pbmc <- sn_find_de(   pbmc,   analysis = "markers",   group_by = "seurat_clusters",   layer = "data",   store_name = "cluster_markers",   return_object = TRUE,   verbose = FALSE )  pbmc <- sn_enrich(   x = pbmc,   source_de_name = "cluster_markers",   gene_clusters = gene ~ cluster,   database = c("GOBP", "H"),   species = "human",   store_name = "cluster_pathways",   pvalue_cutoff = 0.05 ) bulk_de <- sn_find_de(   counts,   metadata = sample_data,   design = ~ batch + condition,   contrast = c("condition", "tumor", "normal"),   method = "auto" )

title: Annotate DE or marker genes by feature class — sn_annotate_de_features
code: sn_annotate_de_features(   x,   de_name = "default",   species = NULL,   gene_col = "gene",   feature_classes = NULL,   resource = c("auto", "msigdbr", "custom"),   custom_resource = NULL,   store_name = NULL,   return_object = inherits(x, "Seurat") )

title: Annotate DE or marker genes by feature class — sn_annotate_de_features
text: x DE/marker data frame, Seurat object containing stored DE result. de_name Stored DE result name x Seurat object. species One "human" "mouse". x Seurat object species NULL, Shennong tries sn_get_species(x). gene_col Column containing gene symbols DE table. feature_classes Feature classes annotate. Defaults "transcription_factor", "surface_membrane", "cytokine", "chemokine". resource Annotation resource. "msigdbr" uses bundled MSigDB gene sets msigdbr; "custom" uses custom_resource; "auto" chooses "custom" custom_resource supplied otherwise "msigdbr". custom_resource Optional data frame least gene feature_class columns. Optional columns species, feature_class_label, feature_class_term, feature_class_source. store_name Optional stored DE result name annotated table x Seurat object return_object = TRUE. Defaults paste0(de_name, "_feature_classes"). return_object TRUE, return updated Seurat object annotated table stored object@misc$de_results[[store_name]]. Otherwise return annotated table.

code: marker_tbl <- tibble::tibble(   gene = c("TBX21", "CXCL10", "IL7R", "ACTB"),   cluster = c("Tcell", "Myeloid", "Tcell", "Bcell"),   avg_log2FC = c(2.1, 1.8, 1.2, 0.4) ) custom_classes <- tibble::tibble(   gene = c("TBX21", "CXCL10", "IL7R"),   feature_class = c("transcription_factor", "chemokine", "surface_membrane") ) sn_annotate_de_features(   marker_tbl,   species = "human",   resource = "custom",   custom_resource = custom_classes ) #> # A tibble: 4 × 11 #>   gene   cluster avg_log2FC feature_classes      feature_class_labels   #>   <chr>  <chr>        <dbl> <chr>                <chr>                  #> 1 TBX21  Tcell          2.1 transcription_factor Transcription factor   #> 2 CXCL10 Myeloid        1.8 chemokine            Chemokine              #> 3 IL7R   Tcell          1.2 surface_membrane     Cell-surface or plasm… #> 4 ACTB   Bcell          0.4 NA                   NA                     #> # ℹ 6 more variables: feature_class_terms <chr>, #> #   feature_class_sources <chr>, is_transcription_factor <lgl>, #> #   is_surface_membrane <lgl>, is_cytokine <lgl>, is_chemokine <lgl>  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ obj <- sn_find_de(obj, analysis = "markers", group_by = "cell_type",   store_name = "celltype_markers", return_object = TRUE ) obj <- sn_annotate_de_features(obj, de_name = "celltype_markers") sn_get_de_result(obj, de_name = "celltype_markers_feature_classes") } # }

title: Assess integration quality across multiple metrics — sn_assess_integration
code: sn_assess_integration(   x,   batch_by = NULL,   label_by = NULL,   cluster_by = NULL,   reduction = .sn_default_metric_reduction(x),   baseline = NULL,   baseline_reduction = .sn_default_baseline_reduction(x, reduction),   graph = NULL,   dims = NULL,   k = 20,   metrics = NULL,   neighbor_method = c("auto", "graph", "annoy", "exact"),   max_cells = 5000,   stratify_by = NULL,   rare_fraction = 0.02,   rare_n = 50,   challenge_threshold = 0.5,   seed = 717,   n_trees = 50 )

title: Assess integration quality across multiple metrics — sn_assess_integration
text: x Seurat object. batch_by Metadata column containing batch labels. label_by Optional metadata column containing biological labels cell type annotations. cluster_by Optional metadata column containing current clustering. reduction Reduction name used primary integrated embedding. Defaults "harmony" present, otherwise "pca". baseline Optional Seurat object used baseline reference PCR batch_by scoring. NULL, baseline taken x. baseline_reduction Optional reduction name used baseline. graph Optional graph name stored x@graphs. dims Optional integer vector embedding dimensions retain. k Number neighbors used graph must built embeddings. metrics Optional character vector selecting metrics compute. Supported values "batch_lisi", "label_lisi", "batch_silhouette", "label_silhouette", "graph_connectivity", "clustering_agreement", "isolated_label_score", "cluster_purity", "cluster_entropy", "pcr_batch", "challenging_groups". neighbor_method Strategy used graph must built. One "auto", "graph", "annoy", "exact". max_cells Optional integer cap used subsample cells running metrics. stratify_by Optional metadata column used preserve representation subsampling. Defaults batch_by. rare_fraction Fraction--cells threshold flagging rare groups. rare_n Absolute cell-count threshold flagging rare groups. challenge_threshold Threshold derived challenge_score used flag difficult groups. seed Random seed used max_cells triggers subsampling. n_trees Number Annoy trees neighbor_method = "annoy".

title: Assess integration quality across multiple metrics — sn_assess_integration
text: list four top-level elements: summary: one row per summary metric, plus aggregate scores. per_cell: per-cell outputs LISI silhouette. per_group: per-group outputs connectivity, composition, difficult-group diagnostics. parameters: effective runtime configuration.

title: Assess integration quality across multiple metrics — sn_assess_integration
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ data("pbmc_small", package = "Shennong") pbmc <- sn_run_cluster(   pbmc_small,   batch = "sample",   species = "human",   verbose = FALSE ) metrics <- sn_assess_integration(   pbmc,   batch = "sample",   cluster_by = "seurat_clusters",   reduction = "harmony",   baseline_reduction = "pca" ) metrics$summary } # }

what: Assess overall QC status and before/after filtering outcomes — sn_assess_qc
title: Assess overall QC status and before/after filtering outcomes — sn_assess_qc
text: function summarizes QC status Seurat object, optionally compares reference object captured filtering, produces per-sample overall quality scores. designed answer whether low-quality cells called doublets removed retaining clean cells, whether ambient-RNA correction introduced problematic zero-count cells.

title: Assess overall QC status and before/after filtering outcomes — sn_assess_qc
code: sn_assess_qc(   object,   reference = NULL,   sample_by = NULL,   store_name = "default",   return_object = FALSE,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Assess overall QC status and before/after filtering outcomes — sn_assess_qc
text: object Seurat object assess. reference Optional Seurat object representing pre-filter state. supplied, function compares retained cells object QC flags doublet calls recorded reference. sample_by Optional metadata column defining samples. NULL, function uses sample orig.ident available otherwise treats object one sample. store_name Name used storing assessment object@misc$qc_assessments. return_object Logical; TRUE, store assessment Seurat object return updated object. verbose Logical; TRUE, print concise QC summary.

title: Assess overall QC status and before/after filtering outcomes — sn_assess_qc
text: list overall, by_sample, comparison, messages return_object = FALSE; otherwise updated Seurat object stored assessment.

title: Assess overall QC status and before/after filtering outcomes — sn_assess_qc
code: if (requireNamespace("Seurat", quietly = TRUE)) {   data("pbmc_small", package = "Shennong")   qc_report <- sn_assess_qc(pbmc_small, verbose = FALSE)   qc_report$overall } #>   n_samples n_cells qc_score qc_label retention_fraction #> 1         2     200 78.06004     good                 NA #>   low_quality_removed_fraction doublet_removed_fraction #> 1                           NA                       NA

what: Check, install, and configure pixi for Shennong Python backends — sn_check_pixi
title: Check, install, and configure pixi for Shennong Python backends — sn_check_pixi
text: Shennong uses pixi optional Python backends scVI/scANVI. R package ships runner scripts, Python environments created user-level Shennong runtime directory ~/.shennong/, inside current analysis project inside installed R package.

title: Check, install, and configure pixi for Shennong Python backends — sn_check_pixi
code: sn_check_pixi(pixi = NULL, quiet = FALSE)  sn_install_pixi(   version = "latest",   pixi_home = "~/.pixi",   bin_dir = NULL,   no_path_update = TRUE,   download_url = NULL,   force = FALSE,   quiet = FALSE )  sn_ensure_pixi(   pixi = NULL,   install = TRUE,   version = "latest",   pixi_home = "~/.pixi",   bin_dir = NULL,   no_path_update = TRUE,   download_url = NULL,   quiet = FALSE )

title: Check, install, and configure pixi for Shennong Python backends — sn_check_pixi
text: pixi Optional pixi executable path. NULL, Shennong checks options("shennong.pixi"), PATH, ~/.pixi/bin/pixi. quiet Logical; suppress status messages possible. version Pixi version passed official installer script. Defaults "latest". pixi_home Pixi home directory. installer defaults "~/.pixi"; Shennong runtime workflows set PIXI_HOME user-level path "~/.shennong/pixi/home". bin_dir Optional directory standalone pixi binary installed. no_path_update Logical; TRUE, ask installer edit shell startup files. download_url Optional custom pixi binary download URL. useful institutional mirrors. force Logical; reinstall even pixi already available. install Logical used sn_ensure_pixi(); install pixi already available.

title: Check, install, and configure pixi for Shennong Python backends — sn_check_pixi
text: sn_check_pixi() sn_ensure_pixi() return named list install status, executable path, version. sn_install_pixi() invisibly returns refreshed check result.

title: Check, install, and configure pixi for Shennong Python backends — sn_check_pixi
code: info <- sn_check_pixi(quiet = TRUE) info$installed #> [1] FALSE if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ sn_ensure_pixi() sn_install_pixi(pixi_home = "~/.pixi", no_path_update = TRUE) } # }

what: Compare sample-level composition between groups — sn_compare_composition
title: Compare sample-level composition between groups — sn_compare_composition
text: Computes sample-level composition categorical variable compares resulting per-sample proportions two groups. avoids treating individual cells independent replicates therefore recommended way estimate composition fold changes significance replicate samples available.

title: Compare sample-level composition between groups — sn_compare_composition
code: sn_compare_composition(   x,   sample_by = NULL,   group_by = NULL,   variable,   contrast,   min_cells = 20,   pseudocount = 0.5,   test = c("wilcox", "none"),   adjust_method = "BH",   additional_cols = NULL,   return_sample_data = FALSE )

title: Compare sample-level composition between groups — sn_compare_composition
text: x Seurat object data frame containing cell-level metadata. sample_by Column defining biological samples. group_by Column defining group condition compare samples. variable Column whose composition compared, example cell type cell-cycle phase. contrast Character vector length 2 giving group levels c(case, control). min_cells Minimum number cells required per sample sample retained comparison. Defaults 20. pseudocount Small value added group means computing log2_fc. Defaults 0.5. test Statistical test apply sample-level proportions. One "wilcox" "none". Defaults "wilcox". adjust_method Multiple-testing correction method passed stats::p.adjust(). Defaults "BH". additional_cols Optional sample-level columns carry intermediate composition table comparison. return_sample_data Logical; TRUE, return summary table completed sample-level composition table.

title: Compare sample-level composition between groups — sn_compare_composition
text: data frame one row per variable level. return_sample_data = TRUE, list summary sample_data returned.

code: sn_deconvolve_bulk(   x,   bulk,   method = c("bayesprism", "cibersortx"),   cell_type_by = NULL,   cell_state_by = NULL,   cell_type_labels = NULL,   cell_state_labels = NULL,   assay = "RNA",   layer = "counts",   bulk_gene_axis = c("auto", "rows", "columns"),   key = NULL,   outdir = tempdir(),   prefix = "deconvolution",   store_name = "default",   cibersortx_result = NULL,   cibersortx_email = NULL,   cibersortx_token = NULL,   cibersortx_container = c("docker", "apptainer"),   cibersortx_container_path = NULL,   cibersortx_dry_run = FALSE,   cibersortx_rmbatch_b_mode = FALSE,   cibersortx_rmbatch_s_mode = FALSE,   cibersortx_perm = 0,   cibersortx_qn = FALSE,   cibersortx_absolute = FALSE,   cibersortx_abs_method = "sig.score",   cibersortx_k_max = 999,   gibbs_control = list(),   opt_control = list(),   n_cores = 1,   update_gibbs = TRUE,   return_object = TRUE )

title: Run bulk RNA-seq deconvolution with single-cell references — sn_deconvolve_bulk
text: x Seurat object used single-cell reference, gene--cell matrix-like object. bulk bulk expression matrix-like object. method One "bayesprism" "cibersortx". cell_type_by Metadata column containing cell-type labels x Seurat object. x matrix, supply cell_type_labels instead. cell_state_by Optional metadata column containing cell-state labels. Defaults cell_type_by. cell_type_labels Optional vector cell-type labels matrix references. cell_state_labels Optional vector cell-state labels matrix references. assay Assay used extract single-cell reference matrix. layer Layer used extract single-cell reference matrix. bulk_gene_axis Orientation genes bulk. Use "rows" common gene--sample layout, "columns" sample--gene input, "auto" infer overlap reference genes. key Optional malignant-cell label_by passed BayesPrism. outdir Output directory used CIBERSORTx file export. prefix Prefix used exported files stored result names. store_name Name used object@misc$deconvolution_results x Seurat object fraction table available. cibersortx_result Optional path completed CIBERSORTx fractions result file import instead running local container. cibersortx_email Optional CIBERSORTx account email. cibersortx_token Optional CIBERSORTx access token. cibersortx_container Container runtime used local execution. One "docker" "apptainer". cibersortx_container_path Optional Apptainer image path. cibersortx_dry_run TRUE, prepare files return local commands without running container. cibersortx_rmbatch_b_mode Whether enable B-mode batch_by correction. cibersortx_rmbatch_s_mode Whether enable S-mode batch_by correction. cibersortx_perm Number permutations used fractions module. cibersortx_qn Whether enable quantile normalization. cibersortx_absolute Whether enable absolute mode. cibersortx_abs_method CIBERSORTx absolute-mode method. cibersortx_k_max Maximum condition number used constructing signature matrix. gibbs_control Optional BayesPrism Gibbs-sampler control list. opt_control Optional BayesPrism optimization control list. n_cores Number cores passed BayesPrism. update_gibbs Whether BayesPrism run final Gibbs update. return_object TRUE x Seurat object, return updated object result table available.

title: Run bulk RNA-seq deconvolution with single-cell references — sn_deconvolve_bulk
text: stored-result list, export-bundle list, updated Seurat object depending selected backend return_object.

title: Run bulk RNA-seq deconvolution with single-cell references — sn_deconvolve_bulk
text: "bayesprism" runs BayesPrism R package locally. "cibersortx" prepares inputs runs local CIBERSORTx container workflow, imports existing fractions result file.

title: Run bulk RNA-seq deconvolution with single-cell references — sn_deconvolve_bulk
code: if (requireNamespace("Seurat", quietly = TRUE)) {   counts <- matrix(rpois(20 * 18, lambda = 3), nrow = 20, ncol = 18)   rownames(counts) <- paste0("gene", seq_len(20))   colnames(counts) <- paste0("cell", seq_len(18))   ref <- sn_initialize_seurat_object(counts, species = "human")   ref$cell_type <- rep(c("Tcell", "Bcell", "Mono"), each = 6)   bulk <- cbind(     sample_a = rowSums(counts[, 1:12, drop = FALSE]),     sample_b = rowSums(counts[, 7:18, drop = FALSE])   )   bundle <- sn_deconvolve_bulk(     ref,     bulk = bulk,     method = "cibersortx",     cell_type_by = "cell_type",     outdir = tempdir(),     cibersortx_email = "demo@example.org",     cibersortx_token = "fake-token",     cibersortx_dry_run = TRUE,     return_object = FALSE   )   names(bundle$files) } #> INFO [2026-07-20 05:53:26] Initializing Seurat object for project: Shennong. #> INFO [2026-07-20 05:53:26] Running QC metrics for human. #> INFO [2026-07-20 05:53:26] Seurat object initialization complete. #> [1] "single_cell_reference" "mixture"               #> [3] "signature_matrix"      "result"

text: object Seurat object. method Rare-cell method. Supported values "gini", "sccad", "sca", "gapclust", "challenging_groups". group_by Optional metadata column used method = "challenging_groups". reduction Reduction used graph-based methods. Defaults "harmony" present, otherwise "pca". dims Optional embedding dimensions use. assay Assay used extract expression values. layer Layer used extract expression values score-based methods. nfeatures Number rare-aware genes use score construction. min_cells Minimum number cells gene must detected considered score-based methods. max_fraction Maximum expressing-cell fraction score-based rare genes. threshold Optional explicit threshold rare-cell score. NULL, function uses upper-IQR rule. k Number neighbors graph-based methods. seed Random seed used stochastic backends. sccad_python Optional Python executable used scCAD backend. sccad_script Optional path upstream scCAD.py script. sccad_normalization Whether scCAD normalize provided matrix internally. sccad_rare_h Rare threshold passed scCAD. sccad_merge_h Merge threshold passed scCAD. sccad_overlap_h Overlap threshold passed scCAD. gapclust_k Upper limit minor-cluster size used GapClust. sca_python Optional Python executable used SCA backend. sca_n_comps Number SCA components used rarity scoring. sca_iters Number SCA iterations. sca_nbhd_size Neighborhood size passed SCA. sca_model Scoring model passed SCA.

what: Run differential expression analysis on a Seurat object — sn_find_de
title: Run differential expression analysis on a Seurat object — sn_find_de
text: function provides single entry point : marker discovery across groups, direct contrasts two groups, pseudobulk contrasts aggregated sample.

title: Run differential expression analysis on a Seurat object — sn_find_de
code: sn_find_de(   object,   analysis = NULL,   ident_1 = NULL,   ident_2 = NULL,   group_by = NULL,   subset_by = NULL,   subset_levels = NULL,   sample_by = NULL,   assay = "RNA",   layer = NULL,   features = NULL,   method = NULL,   only_pos = NULL,   logfc_threshold = 0.1,   min_pct = 0.25,   p_val_cutoff = 0.05,   de_logfc = 0.25,   min_cells_per_sample = 10,   store_name = "default",   return_object = TRUE,   verbose = TRUE,   modality = c("auto", "single_cell", "bulk"),   metadata = NULL,   design = ~condition,   contrast = NULL,   backend_control = list(),   ... )

title: Run differential expression analysis on a Seurat object — sn_find_de
text: object Seurat object. analysis One "markers", "contrast", "pseudobulk". NULL, function infers analysis arguments. ident_1, ident_2 Group labels direct contrasts. required "contrast" "pseudobulk" analyses. group_by Optional metadata column defines groups compare. omitted, Seurat identities used. subset_by Optional metadata column used repeat contrast within subset, example per cell type. subset_levels Optional character vector subset values analyze. Defaults observed values subset_by. sample_by Metadata column containing sample IDs. Required "pseudobulk" analyses. assay Assay used DE analysis. Defaults "RNA". layer Assay layer used DE analysis. Defaults "data" marker contrast analyses "counts" pseudobulk analyses. features Optional feature subset test. method Statistical method. "markers" "contrast", can Seurat test.use value "COSGR" marker discovery. "pseudobulk", choose one "DESeq2", "edgeR", "limma". bulk input, choose "auto", "edger", "deseq2", "limma", "dream". only_pos Whether return positive markers. Defaults TRUE "markers" FALSE otherwise. logfc_threshold, min_pct Standard Seurat marker filtering arguments. p_val_cutoff Adjusted p-value threshold used storing result metadata. de_logfc Absolute log fold-change threshold used storing result metadata. min_cells_per_sample Minimum cells required sample/group pseudobulk profile retained. store_name Key used object@misc$de_results. return_object TRUE, return updated Seurat object stored DE results. Otherwise return result table. verbose Whether emit progress information. modality Input modality. "auto" selects single-cell analysis Seurat objects bulk analysis matrices, lists, SummarizedExperiment objects. metadata Optional sample metadata bulk analysis. design fixed- mixed-effects formula bulk analysis. contrast Character triple giving bulk contrast variable, numerator, denominator. backend_control Bulk backend controls custom bulk runner/precomputed result. ... Additional arguments passed selected DE method.

title: Run differential expression analysis on a Seurat object — sn_find_de
text: single-cell input, either DE result table updated Seurat object. bulk input, validated Shennong bulk-DE result.

title: Run differential expression analysis on a Seurat object — sn_find_de
text: Results can stored back object@misc$de_results[[store_name]], downstream helpers sn_plot_dot() can reuse marker table.

title: Run differential expression analysis on a Seurat object — sn_find_de
code: if (requireNamespace("Seurat", quietly = TRUE)) {   counts <- matrix(rpois(20 * 24, lambda = 1), nrow = 20, ncol = 24)   rownames(counts) <- c(     paste0("GENE", 1:14),     "CD3D", "CD3E", "TRAC", "MS4A1", "CD79A", "HLA-DRA"   )   colnames(counts) <- paste0("cell", 1:24)   counts[c("CD3D", "CD3E", "TRAC"), 1:12] <-     counts[c("CD3D", "CD3E", "TRAC"), 1:12] + 20   counts[c("MS4A1", "CD79A", "HLA-DRA"), 13:24] <-     counts[c("MS4A1", "CD79A", "HLA-DRA"), 13:24] + 20    obj <- sn_initialize_seurat_object(counts, species = "human")   obj$cell_type <- rep(c("Tcell", "Bcell"), each = 12)   Seurat::Idents(obj) <- obj$cell_type   obj <- Seurat::NormalizeData(obj, verbose = FALSE)    marker_tbl <- sn_find_de(     obj,     analysis = "markers",     group_by = "cell_type",     layer = "data",     min_pct = 0,     logfc_threshold = 0,     return_object = FALSE,     verbose = FALSE   )   head(marker_tbl)    obj <- sn_find_de(     obj,     analysis = "markers",     group_by = "cell_type",     layer = "data",     min_pct = 0,     logfc_threshold = 0,     store_name = "celltype_markers",     return_object = TRUE,     verbose = FALSE   )   names(obj@misc$de_results) } #> INFO [2026-07-20 05:53:31] Initializing Seurat object for project: Shennong. #> INFO [2026-07-20 05:53:31] Running QC metrics for human. #> INFO [2026-07-20 05:53:31] Seurat object initialization complete. #> For a (much!) faster implementation of the Wilcoxon Rank Sum Test, #> (default method for FindMarkers) please install the presto package #> -------------------------------------------- #> install.packages('devtools') #> devtools::install_github('immunogenomics/presto') #> -------------------------------------------- #> After installation of presto, Seurat will automatically use the more  #> efficient implementation (no further action necessary). #> This message will be shown once per session #> [1] "celltype_markers"  bulk_counts <- matrix(   stats::rpois(40 * 6, 20), nrow = 40,   dimnames = list(paste0("gene_", 1:40), paste0("sample_", 1:6)) ) bulk_metadata <- data.frame(   condition = factor(rep(c("control", "treated"), each = 3)),   row.names = colnames(bulk_counts) ) bulk_de <- sn_find_de(   bulk_counts,   metadata = bulk_metadata,   design = ~condition,   contrast = c("condition", "treated", "control"),   backend_control = list(result = data.frame(     gene = rownames(bulk_counts), logFC = 0, PValue = 1, FDR = 1   )) )

title: Find doublets using scDblFinder — sn_find_doublets
text: function identifies potential doublets Seurat object converting SingleCellExperiment using scDblFinder package.

title: Find doublets using scDblFinder — sn_find_doublets
code: sn_find_doublets(   object,   clusters = NULL,   group_by = NULL,   dbr_sd = NULL,   ncores = 1,   assay = "RNA",   layer = "counts",   min_features = 200 )

title: Find doublets using scDblFinder — sn_find_doublets
text: object Seurat object. clusters Optional cluster assignments. provided, scDblFinder attempt automatic clustering. group_by optional metadata column used donor sample grouping. dbr_sd numeric value adjusting doublet rate; see scDblFinder documentation. ncores Number cores use (parallel processing). assay Assay used doublet detection. Defaults "RNA". layer Layer used input count matrix. Defaults "counts". min_features Minimum number detected features required cell passed scDblFinder(). Defaults 200. Cells threshold skipped retain NA output columns.

title: Find doublets using scDblFinder — sn_find_doublets
text: input Seurat object two new columns meta.data: scDblFinder.class scDblFinder.score layer = "counts", scDblFinder.class_corrected scDblFinder.score_corrected non-default corrected layers. Cells whose selected layer sums zero whose detected-feature count min_features skipped retain NA corresponding output columns.

title: Find doublets using scDblFinder — sn_find_doublets

what: Identify rare or difficult-to-separate groups — sn_identify_challenging_groups
title: Identify rare or difficult-to-separate groups — sn_identify_challenging_groups
text: helper summarizes group size, local neighbor purity, graph connectivity, silhouette width grouping column. intended surface rare populations poorly separated groups may obscured standard UMAP visual inspection.

title: Identify rare or difficult-to-separate groups — sn_identify_challenging_groups
code: sn_identify_challenging_groups(   x,   group_by = NULL,   graph = NULL,   reduction = .sn_default_metric_reduction(x),   dims = NULL,   cells = NULL,   k = 20,   neighbor_method = c("auto", "graph", "annoy", "exact"),   max_cells = 3000,   stratify_by = NULL,   rare_fraction = 0.02,   rare_n = 50,   challenge_threshold = 0.5,   seed = 717,   n_trees = 50 )

title: Identify rare or difficult-to-separate groups — sn_identify_challenging_groups
text: x Seurat object. group_by Metadata column defining groups inspect. graph Optional graph name stored x@graphs. NULL, function tries reuse existing nearest-neighbor graph falls back kNN graph built selected embedding. reduction Reduction name used graph must built embeddings. Defaults "harmony" present, otherwise "pca". dims Optional integer vector embedding dimensions retain. cells Optional character vector cell names include. k Number neighbors used building graph embeddings. neighbor_method Strategy used graph must built. One "auto", "graph", "annoy", "exact". max_cells Optional integer cap used subsample cells running metric. stratify_by Optional metadata column used preserve representation subsampling. Defaults group_by. rare_fraction Fraction--cells threshold flagging rare groups. rare_n Absolute cell-count threshold flagging rare groups. challenge_threshold Threshold derived challenge_score used flag difficult groups. seed Random seed used max_cells triggers subsampling. n_trees Number Annoy trees neighbor_method = "annoy".

title: Identify rare or difficult-to-separate groups — sn_identify_challenging_groups

title: Install Shennong from CRAN, GitHub, or a local source — sn_install_shennong
text: channel One "auto", "cran", "github", "local". package Package name. Defaults "Shennong". source Installation source. channel = "github", supply "owner/repo" string; omitted, Shennong uses "zerostwo/shennong". channel = "local", supply local package directory source tarball path. ref GitHub ref used channel = "github". Defaults "main". repos CRAN-like repositories used install.packages(). ... Additional arguments passed utils::install.packages() remotes::install_github() / remotes::install_local(). GitHub installs, Shennong defaults dependencies = FALSE upgrade = "never" unless override explicitly.

what: Interpret cluster markers for cell-type annotation — sn_interpret_annotation
title: Interpret cluster markers for cell-type annotation — sn_interpret_annotation
text: Interpret cluster markers cell-type annotation

title: Interpret cluster markers for cell-type annotation — sn_interpret_annotation
code: sn_interpret_annotation(   object,   de_name = NULL,   cluster_by = NULL,   n_markers = 10,   marker_selection = c("specific", "top"),   enrichment_name = NULL,   n_terms = 5,   enrichment_selection = c("specific", "top"),   include_qc = TRUE,   reduction = "umap",   n_neighbor_clusters = 3,   background = NULL,   annotation_mode = c("single_pass", "agentic"),   output_format = c("llm", "human"),   provider = NULL,   model = NULL,   reasoning_effort = NULL,   include_json_schema = TRUE,   apply_metadata = TRUE,   metadata_prefix = "sn_annotation",   metadata_fields = c("primary_label", "broad_label", "confidence", "status",     "risk_flags"),   label_candidates = NULL,   label_style = c("title", "snake", "asis"),   return_prompt = FALSE,   store_name = "default",   return_object = TRUE,   show_progress = interactive(),   ... )

title: Interpret cluster markers for cell-type annotation — sn_interpret_annotation
text: object Seurat object. de_name Optional stored marker-result name. omitted, Shennong prefers "default", single available result, otherwise recent marker result. cluster_by Metadata column containing cluster labels. n_markers Number top markers per cluster. marker_selection choose marker genes annotation evidence: "specific" prefers genes relatively unique one cluster, "top" keeps raw top-ranked genes. enrichment_name Optional stored enrichment result used add cluster-level functional evidence. n_terms Number enrichment terms per cluster enrichment_name supplied. enrichment_selection choose pathway/function terms annotation evidence: "specific" prefers terms concentrated fewer clusters, "top" keeps raw top-ranked terms. include_qc Logical; whether include cluster-level QC summaries evidence bundle. reduction Optional dimensional reduction name used summarize cluster neighborhood geometry, example "umap". Use NULL disable geometry evidence. n_neighbor_clusters Number nearest clusters report reduction centroid distances. background Optional study-specific background information provide additional interpretation context. annotation_mode Annotation workflow mode. "single_pass" sends one compact prompt. "agentic" runs broad-pass annotation followed focused refinement pass ambiguous ILC-relevant clusters, reconciles result canonical lineage hints. output_format One "llm" model-ready prompt bundle "human" human-readable summary. provider Optional model provider function. left NULL, Shennong try construct ellmer-backed provider OPENAI_API_KEY plus optional OPENAI_BASE_URL OPENAI_MODEL environment variables. model Optional model identifier. reasoning_effort Optional reasoning effort forwarded compatible GPT-5 chat-completions providers, example "minimal", "low", "medium", "high", "xhigh" upstream endpoint supports . include_json_schema Logical; whether request structured JSON output provider. Defaults TRUE annotation workflows. apply_metadata Logical; TRUE structured annotation response returned, map cluster labels back onto cell Seurat metadata. metadata_prefix Prefix used metadata columns written back Seurat object apply_metadata = TRUE. metadata_fields Annotation fields write back Seurat metadata apply_metadata = TRUE. Defaults core fields : primary_label, broad_label, confidence, status, risk_flags. Detailed evidence remains stored interpretation table object@misc. label_candidates Optional vector candidate cell-type labels broad lineages treated annotation priors sorted enriched datasets. injected prompt constraints, model may still return broader label evidence weak. label_style Naming style used normalize returned cell-type labels. One "title", "snake", "asis". return_prompt TRUE, return prompt bundle without calling provider. store_name Name used object@misc$interpretation_results. return_object TRUE, return updated Seurat object. show_progress Logical; TRUE, emit step-wise progress logs , cli available, console progress bar waiting LLM response. ... Additional arguments forwarded provider.

title: Interpret cluster markers for cell-type annotation — sn_interpret_annotation
text: prompt bundle, response, updated Seurat object.

title: Interpret cluster markers for cell-type annotation — sn_interpret_annotation
code: if (requireNamespace("Seurat", quietly = TRUE)) {   counts <- matrix(rpois(20 * 24, lambda = 1), nrow = 20, ncol = 24)   rownames(counts) <- c(     paste0("GENE", 1:14),     "CD3D", "CD3E", "TRAC", "MS4A1", "CD79A", "HLA-DRA"   )   colnames(counts) <- paste0("cell", 1:24)   counts[c("CD3D", "CD3E", "TRAC"), 1:12] <-     counts[c("CD3D", "CD3E", "TRAC"), 1:12] + 20   counts[c("MS4A1", "CD79A", "HLA-DRA"), 13:24] <-     counts[c("MS4A1", "CD79A", "HLA-DRA"), 13:24] + 20   obj <- sn_initialize_seurat_object(counts, species = "human")   obj$cell_type <- rep(c("Tcell", "Bcell"), each = 12)   Seurat::Idents(obj) <- obj$cell_type   obj <- Seurat::NormalizeData(obj, verbose = FALSE)   obj <- sn_find_de(obj, analysis = "markers", group_by = "cell_type",     layer = "data", min_pct = 0, logfc_threshold = 0,     store_name = "celltype_markers", return_object = TRUE, verbose = FALSE   )   prompt <- sn_interpret_annotation(     obj,     de_name = "celltype_markers",     cluster_by = "cell_type",     return_prompt = TRUE   )   prompt$task } #> INFO [2026-07-20 05:53:36] Initializing Seurat object for project: Shennong. #> INFO [2026-07-20 05:53:36] Running QC metrics for human. #> INFO [2026-07-20 05:53:36] Seurat object initialization complete. #> INFO [2026-07-20 05:53:37] [sn_interpret_annotation] Starting interpretation workflow. #> INFO [2026-07-20 05:53:37] [sn_interpret_annotation] Step 1/5: Preparing annotation evidence (elapsed 0.0s). #> As of Seurat v5, we recommend using AggregateExpression to perform pseudo-bulk analysis. #> This message is displayed once per session. #> INFO [2026-07-20 05:53:37] [sn_interpret_annotation] Step 2/5: Building annotation prompt (elapsed 0.1s). #> INFO [2026-07-20 05:53:37] [sn_interpret_annotation] Prompt prepared (total elapsed 0.1s). #> [1] "annotation"

code: if (requireNamespace("Seurat", quietly = TRUE)) {   counts <- matrix(rpois(10 * 12, lambda = 1), nrow = 10, ncol = 12)   rownames(counts) <- c(     "CD3D", "CD3E", "TRAC", "LTB", "MS4A1",     "CD79A", "HLA-DRA", "LYZ", "ACTB", "MALAT1"   )   colnames(counts) <- paste0("cell", 1:12)   obj <- sn_initialize_seurat_object(counts, species = "human")   obj <- Seurat::NormalizeData(obj, verbose = FALSE)   obj <- sn_find_de(     obj,     analysis = "markers",     group_by = NULL,     layer = "data",     min_pct = 0,     logfc_threshold = 0,     return_object = TRUE,     verbose = FALSE   )   sn_list_results(obj) } #> INFO [2026-07-20 05:53:51] Initializing Seurat object for project: Shennong. #> INFO [2026-07-20 05:53:51] Running QC metrics for human. #> INFO [2026-07-20 05:53:51] Seurat object initialization complete. #> Warning: No DE genes identified #> Warning: The following tests were not performed:  #> Warning: When testing Shennong versus all: #> 	Cells in one or both identity groups are not present in the data requested #> # A tibble: 1 × 8 #>   collection type  name    analysis method created_at     n_rows source #>   <chr>      <chr> <chr>   <chr>    <chr>  <chr>           <int> <chr>  #> 1 de_results de    default markers  wilcox 2026-07-20 05…      0 NA
